name: Unite the Country
subject: Tonight is the next #DemDebate in Nevada!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Unite the Country, [www
body:
#
Peter,
With just days to go until the Nevada Caucus, tonight is another Democratic Debate! We're excited to cheer Joe Biden on at tonight's debate in Nevada.

More polls released this week continue to show Joe Biden has the best chance at defeating Donald Trump in November. As President Obama said, Joe Biden is what so many others pretend to be. He's a leader with integrity who has a record of getting things done. He will unite our country -- and he'll be ready on day one to get to work.

Our team at Unite the Country will be live-tweeting and sharing Joe's best moments during the #DemDebate -- and we need your help to make sure we get Joe's message to as many voters as possible before Nevadans go to the caucuses on Saturday.

**Will you help support Joe Biden tonight?[Follow us on Twitter at @UniteCountryPAC](https://act.unitethecountry.com/page/m/46df76c1/287b9173/2b05c0f4/7e766ed8/435290987/VEsH/?g=x7_OMVH8x8VdxQ307JVTQtA "https://twitter.com/unitecountrypac"), retweet your favorite clips & quotes we share from the debate, and tweet why you're think Joe Biden is the best candidate using the hashtags #UniteForBiden and #DemDebate! **

We're excited to support Joe Biden tonight as we have during the previous debates. Follow along as we share videos, graphics and quotes of Joe's top moments. We'll be reminding voters that Joe is the best candidate to defeat Donald Trump and how he's ready on day one to lead.

Tune in tonight at 9 pm E.T. and join us on Twitter as we live-tweet the #DemDebate!

See you tonight,

Unite the Country

P.S. We are also organizing Joe Biden's supporters online! If you'd like to join our Slack group, please reply to this email.

[](https://act.unitethecountry.com/page/m/46df76c1/287b9173/2b05c0f4/7e766ed9/435290987/VEsE/?g=x7_OMVH8x8VdxQ307JVTQtA "https://facebook.com/unitethecountrypac") [](https://act.unitethecountry.com/page/m/46df76c1/287b9173/2b05c0f4/7e766ed8/435290987/VEsF/?g=x7_OMVH8x8VdxQ307JVTQtA "https://twitter.com/unitecountrypac") [](https://act.unitethecountry.com/page/m/46df76c1/287b9173/2b05c0f4/7e766eda/435290987/VEsC/?g=x7_OMVH8x8VdxQ307JVTQtA "https://www.instagram.com/unitethecountry/")
* * *
Paid for by Unite the Country, [www.unitethecountry.com](https://act.unitethecountry.com/page/m/46df76c1/287b9173/2b05c0f4/7e766edb/435290987/VEsD/?g=x7_OMVH8x8VdxQ307JVTQtA "http://www.unitethecountry.com"), and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.
---
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. We're counting on supporters like you, but if you want to unsubscribe, you can [click here](https://act.unitethecountry.com/page/m/46df76c1/287b9173/2b05c0f4/7e766ec4/435290987/VEsA/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/?g=x7_OMVH8x8VdxQ307JVTQtA "https://https://unitethecountry.cp.bsd.net/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com").

name: Holcomb Crew
subject: Take a trip through Indiana’s past
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Eric Holcomb for Indiana
body:
This week on Holcomb's One Indiana, Eric’s covering some of the best historic sites in Indiana! [ ](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=f281e354e933421fb41d1ba736284311&_e=d9cBSkvfXmGumq_XQy6S4z_Vx9dA3v8sflJXbdIyrxCIBd6pLwU5xEV4AC4R8xVCPNa-VBRiBV7tdT1e4WPEFwPsRSCRBzY0Qrraj7ozhcXkdC_Bb3ExAsfXjAqH-0H4H951bt73O0-jLamS0_6fmDOMwLFa82gCyLgTX5-XiDbThCtDDssuLVPllZiRmfOGXgpM6nctEr5VFm7_ajw7HZKtJ4uHGPkFb4Z0YNrNCJDXSqSViMUbKrmZNoCQF_QU5zMICGtkyLT6R370o7oSBOKAJ75Df1XgcdCZSrz0ZnvNuiF0455tIPEYTJpCitKYCX_DaPJYVffjovejonr7vGPXQfnsz-DkUlB4tO1-jfqUfNsOaz5aKZ3LRlzdvvAJy8BEAWFzlR_tB1YwwECxsSFbUkIKJlwQUS2A-ladOuk%3D) Friend,

This week on Holcomb's One Indiana, Eric’s talking about a subject he loves.... Indiana’s history!
Presidents’ Day was on Monday and, in honor of the historical holiday, Eric wanted to share with you a few of his favorite historic sites throughout our state -- many with connections to Indiana’s presidential history! Eric’s a history buff and this list could’ve been much, much longer.
So here’s just a sampling of some great Hoosier history sites! [ ](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=f281e354e933421fb41d1ba736284311&_e=d9cBSkvfXmGumq_XQy6S4z_Vx9dA3v8sflJXbdIyrxCIBd6pLwU5xEV4AC4R8xVCPNa-VBRiBV7tdT1e4WPEFwPsRSCRBzY0Qrraj7ozhcXkdC_Bb3ExAsfXjAqH-0H4H951bt73O0-jLamS0_6fmDOMwLFa82gCyLgTX5-XiDbThCtDDssuLVPllZiRmfOGXgpM6nctEr5VFm7_ajw7HQ-ztMSdU5UlPnqgFSA6PgIZHAmTik5S0uNgVwZ5G9Wdei9vWDPiL_nra0kIdr1MdRC8MVA9CU2JI_iugFUI6u3M6c_tyfZkQSPT79NBoU5-VNEQMwGJiHXbklPqR2IE-zHEBGovUYXBhpkDa2egG2yqWAoyYbE8a2MyxaqP7RZ5SwE6tJGvB2TXk8huiPU5XvIpvCIDXUoXUWSJG2RTkcw%3D)
* **[Abe Lincoln’s Boyhood Home](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=f281e354e933421fb41d1ba736284311&_e=d9cBSkvfXmGumq_XQy6S46gnO1ZrqcTxL8jcqrO3Zb2s_YaqWU8BYeV8sMIN_jSNhFS8EDKCJ7tmb3tznbr2xw9un11dd1Dl_y4Hg39vs-Blj4bvAY3bFV3oIO2PcTTB1EjDkQlL_Sf5GXyEEzjDypxmG3ueXWi9GyT2aHPCw_c6MtETbdqK-xr2J4SlxRfJd-2ohOuZ6PP_FAPHX-KSglFLuoL6QIVt6bjPo-rocS7JT_YAiinB9xvD2Q3vI6o86B6I5-ShVnozL2TAPBQDM1kRFZZVIplLGj3aKcNHkASICXoCNXzYn4sx8SbVZn9i676JdIb_E3htGjLtm2yyxTTc1Lua0yo9b5FzX4IqIYRl6tSUVnm5yBf9hcPK4otVnGq2tTKqX1rmlFyytQ40lg%3D%3D)** (Spencer County) - Kentucky may be the birthplace of Lincoln, and Illinois claims to be the land of Lincoln, but Indiana is really the soul of Lincoln. His values were shaped during the formative years he spent in Southern Indiana.
* **[Angel Mounds](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=f281e354e933421fb41d1ba736284311&_e=d9cBSkvfXmGumq_XQy6S44z2d8S_OXNhZJZ_WGZTnuKZWQYuMLDyOjU78KC0C42nqkkhK_zHW1S9r7nA4DCPPTBqkRZOLX1sYuqYeO9SKM8PGyMqXdfNyBYWHUgD70N3ENcXHM0nY3cQBIbyFUbRl4cer_jqlL2gAzACfNQ9IIs_RKC4vP1H0nhH43GlwWGiXJ__mMK-BTsLBQ8ZOi-bwgA66bWJbdyrF5l_eEJyxgjmnYfqn5RPlXodDa-m-dYzAUhCO53MamCmD7fWguf55_ZTPIXVXu5n3mwqWbIJagNcH2rIZdtzOvDVlH5QwGfbboxzDHDJva71HBW2VnkHdrGRZIyxAFC-RHLcaAqnHL9XgXcECx44X75-kMVbTV6ToADJE7vUn5Y0CZ9n2bJ8oE0nHeJ64LT-JGNX5sWkdMhmzGJmnV0rVS6sRja4tAx9)** (Vanderburgh and Warrick counties) - uncover Indiana’s Native American roots!
* **[Benjamin Harrison Home](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=f281e354e933421fb41d1ba736284311&_e=d9cBSkvfXmGumq_XQy6S47q__4SLnJ4xYvRZ3COXign-w6f_O-_vYWCZEoWodFfnCR4itRxX_YBZnG5lVnN8tD6qu4pn3Hxttkn4k2FxOxMBCYirX8GjDG7eeODxTwKLC24tFq_F9H7Q_4AfYuJ_v6UVds2KqG2_Ef-duJ9Fc_RUopAHL0vKLLK5izqTl9HozxzaTlbP7z3-i5rPL7MYgoVqmajlz-j_2tzKdO8BWLWpzgtpsHn-cHSDbagr9AOYPmGzLf-cNxybycm3gpsjXWHqPsmmVKspSVK8gt71TH-d4xyMZfzCx_O2tbzjQtTARTCCxMzfnJAmKcJOxkS_sQZBAG3FTAWGgW2r7FZmpO6tFsqBnixqstHBmd_N8izg)** (Marion County) - come take a walk through the home of the 23rd president. You can even catch a play in the candlelight theatre!
* **[Corydon](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=f281e354e933421fb41d1ba736284311&_e=d9cBSkvfXmGumq_XQy6S4xt_q7uir8e851HUx8BCA0G_I9ZcDjce4OIMr-k3nu5_kHoi-T9eic-4V9YRnzFQHM-4DKCXmU79wWE5Di8SftsI8ujae-6P7epfj108sPIM09neW6r6yqcQRCPCt9u6XtjlQ5fbXLQ73ZmqK9TgEP0SUCMPqg6Xwby9Ob0PvBDyfMDK7FA7O6bFGSr354dIxg5YZWpssTKtRZN1MrFZKGBsBemGQAPkKsqEvOz9pFfIQMND6TExFt0r4ATl_4OYQzz27dib3ccmkGQ2aBL5E3MAUyly7KZThE7BVODDwK5OuSAnsdAGVHtQDQWZzwx9PRqRyOjHja4xR6jrbvwFuUjB_kLt73g_jB-ebOJa-Dq_QGSdT7kzTaRRAL9v8b6S6rG0NixW4BwXuC2kjkkruv8%3D)** (Harrison County) - the site of Indiana’s first capital, Indiana’s first constitutional convention, and the only Civil War battle on Indiana soil!
* **[Gennett Records Walk of Fame](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=f281e354e933421fb41d1ba736284311&_e=d9cBSkvfXmGumq_XQy6S470rdeui18MUjQdzRq6D0VkaNjcPuao0GMVjREg3bx_p4RL4F8nPnD8uxwlorID5OfFaTK6fiJzgVhi8nO56YTm8DUihP8D44aJ4Vcfx2_J7WJnjBC6lmrTFkJt9O-Ex_b8YIlqbAz0FmsFnSlSnrVXGll67AN48UblFVJE6rAT8O_8_rk39anWIwJeUwuQgQjR5L3CaZbCngoGbGMiMa9dEpGqmPx11xMMP56p2eBzSX15tpPYtgOidi9keYifpWIlxApcHNJEOAHbIQklW4NGxiArQ7Kl_Yd-6dCcyoKn6idnzdE7AIdbF8-bhsWiisdxGWpc4VvDnoVxboohUPl9PKiBo1JQ7uBVA1E-mVgWmCmql1xcbpkbUeZtVmikWcH-oms_98hpg7rQRn-A7JR4%3D)** (Wayne County) - come and see the studio that recorded Louis Armstrong, Hoagy Carmichael, and other music greats!
* **[George Rogers Clark Memorial](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=f281e354e933421fb41d1ba736284311&_e=d9cBSkvfXmGumq_XQy6S41ubm1dZ1k9CKtP26MP8jFz9BdjCV4ntlVLp8ZDp9Myg9VpQbNJwoihbb61q45x1ewFJbed6blpZwh5SIUmTLOXDduYkOX_eLdwN-9uXpb1Zvla_xzQu5NOv3N0v-tsdqNoZXC5KxXsRfMuerx_yo9Lj6AGjLXrUWXpxzfchCNv3iGGTRwklLZ1-8FEu-ak9gG8qSzHBKGCm_CszI3oa0LEHaqFP1NpCCOXR_TachEVZBPZWuxF8xht8Q4zMgCvD8mclHTnzYeyxdo86ODnEcOe1LM-dtRPSjWbl_jwGcm1vqqPvJVHycXasPaPiAd36HBXDezZYFkOQ4dHxycH0So25tF6NSgTQuzArRSzXv54lyTRVi22emWLRap94zFkPzg%3D%3D)** (Knox County) - relive the battle that gave America control of the frontier in 1779 and made George Rogers Clark into a Hoosier legend!
* **[Levi Coffin House](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=f281e354e933421fb41d1ba736284311&_e=d9cBSkvfXmGumq_XQy6S4_9c6IbFB5pWsUi0iZ3-wGtIwayy6-pt_1J7jqwz3Qohb9e4yqFX5Feq4j4iop_LndYewWCG4OmDM35uzoK6H5FB-hO-Nn3t87VGm0g1RxwyOMlq3-UAoh1fNCwAc8qwxnB9-A_vfZZpBIyYPDGIuuOiYdxxvHOU4OzPLA5MxrEPi9meuDIKiFSq5qup4inlh4euKrxVBo7DqxHRzaoY6XA9Ulgi0_XQ28wecxDuiVWwpUcpMRNtKU0YY6t1Umwv5NoVPAYPeiHZk8QD6TGA8tvqFgO_wAdNHJaLzPdSKXX9hjzC1QepkV8pSa1zSxVtwymBziBaFl6VmkTLN0rpWCa2HOiE3jAQd4PY5Qym4rZqtYSunMyAyeH4He4H4vF0yw%3D%3D)** (Wayne County) - a registered National Historic Landmark, the Levi Coffin House was “Grand Central Station” on the Underground Railroad!
* **[New Harmony](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=f281e354e933421fb41d1ba736284311&_e=d9cBSkvfXmGumq_XQy6S4-iU-Nmlm2tFunoMPU7uVKM7I62dqRsyUFf-J-5H-u-IXvYJBAMO6w3kWx8hltWIgW2qGliNBRQniaSNGxo6qNwRokUdcZp7B-7hVmU8sOZ_iAB782_d999kzNGDgSa7lwZxIEAyF_rQiZ3290oKoY8k4zaImVNsSCWeTrQTHKo4cEVFNDLULvP5xhjfY3_cr7vCZzOEu83aaHic64-OsdS7oUdDp39mVFOHWwR7UZ64D9z05OWNDcEQPfbT7S75zyzdxzCefOQWHqLVH8iGkhCGkpkTR7Q8mKBoBFRsHmkZBcw1NnieWjx_EI9WU1enEXgwBJR8HsZBmBpd3qvU5W5MSqG7svN6FKOi06RWGlK4)** (Posey County) - the most beautiful gardens in Indiana. You won’t want to miss out!
* **[Tippecanoe Battlefield](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=f281e354e933421fb41d1ba736284311&_e=d9cBSkvfXmGumq_XQy6S4y59pPV150ZmT9uF3lSs14iCNFlbNhIi59RoQAtNN_Vx09CyOvSSwniywredAvgRt4aO66ntrWu3uXdLCaXaDlYXmmO3quh_VGW_dz4xgfXYudJNSVpeHpLl8SMQ4TSlmOek0uCzgpdy530TBXdC_HbUMxVtnVUjx1UESgDjwG681MNAu7-D09Dbxs1eCsU4Aiyi5QQvz9FJPW95_ISkLOlr9NiRxdR7nQoHw5NALzRn6gwkKFWx3S8VYMOkKuKgFA2GFPHjOocPp9lqvRjXOj4eSuuq-FZGmXCKpihmXopgUGj-iu4Okmo0dcwdocALnCIOv66p5qUKZbhcowb3RgHXuoUpTsRgzmGs1Fw0P1jeYlJC0HsSrh1Lyyws3d8X19cDZb14oZSoV8Xf88UglVSP9R_CE1a2YxhHzu5esheV)** (Tippecanoe County) - you can take a hike through the battlefield where William Henry Harrison and Tecumseh met in 1811.
* **[Vincennes and Grouseland](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=f281e354e933421fb41d1ba736284311&_e=Bb5EJ3TrXapI-Ntseq3vlqf3zvjj0IxvzRvgVzImVvyarYBEHpYOq_eKUG9Os5Dr9qO1B1Cg24PeZ7ZW78VryoJP1rAxk8MeuvTkmOJLIV_h5KsdBQL8QMb10iEb1gydRKsvsJGWu8mQHHDhTlA1k72ytCfXPpPACj8X3qcfNJtaA39laHftXg5hJgY6zHkdB4eiL0zQakNWvdxldfzPPpliHLwQEbaxRgWAT8_IoSpX2YiDlmVIIVaGrWYIyBIBhb8xy5OaJuVGOKLZyqZNfyordauBBhjdyxLuNV0gXcS26zAkmWJh-rF5jd_8oc1o6F3g90ExlYm-vlNpu9vqG5gSO4qkraj9V8pB2olTcm8uolW6G5wIZpVFtfIyM4rjqL103opiNbcpuWMC_rfT9Q%3D%3D)** (Knox County) - William Henry Harrison’s home when he was governor of Indiana, this historic mansion contains memorabilia from his first campaign and much more.

**Flash Survey** **Q: Where does your next Hoosier history road trip take you?** [ **Let us know!** ](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=f281e354e933421fb41d1ba736284311&_e=d9cBSkvfXmGumq_XQy6S4z_Vx9dA3v8sflJXbdIyrxCIBd6pLwU5xEV4AC4R8xVCPNa-VBRiBV7tdT1e4WPEFwPsRSCRBzY0Qrraj7ozhcXkdC_Bb3ExAsfXjAqH-0H4H951bt73O0-jLamS0_6fmDOMwLFa82gCyLgTX5-XiDbThCtDDssuLVPllZiRmfOGXgpM6nctEr5VFm7_ajw7HTHlVxOA1Ol4H7fbyV6I3CHOMHaEdtGoXTN8pFPfWm_ZxiTYm0IQMGUKS93Msj0owgSc7cufb_GU9vzs6T6wHonbujxlbv5914hYIRyxLoC1bYl3QXems1MDrlW1KWYqa4LGGw6ni3bsPenNS4T4WY_piinl71FQ1Wqk3YhTB_hX_cXKZFfsA-2NsrQHCLnZQUl1uv0HUyIOkYwk3DI9EM4%3D) **That’s our list for this week! **Governor Holcomb loves our Hoosier history and he’s glad to share some of his favorites with you! clean_body: This week on Holcomb's One Indiana, Eric’s covering some of the best historic sites in Indiana! Friend, This week on Holcomb's One Indiana, Eric’s talking about a subject he loves.... Indiana’s history! Presidents’ Day was on Monday and, in honor of the historical holiday, Eric wanted to share with you a few of his favorite historic sites throughout our state -- many with connections to Indiana’s presidential history! Eric’s a history buff and this list could’ve been much, much longer. So here’s just a sampling of some great Hoosier history sites! (Spencer County) - Kentucky may be the birthplace of Lincoln, and Illinois claims to be the land of Lincoln, but Indiana is really the soul of Lincoln. His values were shaped during the formative years he spent in Southern Indiana. (Vanderburgh and Warrick counties) - uncover Indiana’s Native American roots! (Marion County) - come take a walk through the home of the 23rd president. You can even catch a play in the candlelight theatre! (Harrison County) - the site of Indiana’s first capital, Indiana’s first constitutional convention, and the only Civil War battle on Indiana soil! (Wayne County) - come and see the studio that recorded Louis Armstrong, Hoagy Carmichael, and other music greats! (Knox County) - relive the battle that gave America control of the frontier in 1779 and made George Rogers Clark into a Hoosier legend! (Wayne County) - a registered National Historic Landmark, the Levi Coffin House was “Grand Central Station” on the Underground Railroad! (Posey County) - the most beautiful gardens in Indiana. You won’t want to miss out! (Tippecanoe County) - you can take a hike through the battlefield where William Henry Harrison and Tecumseh met in 1811. (Knox County) - William Henry Harrison’s home when he was governor of Indiana, this historic mansion contains memorabilia from his first campaign and much more. Flash Survey Q: Where does your next Hoosier history road trip take you? That’s our list for this week! Governor Holcomb loves our Hoosier history and he’s glad to share some of his favorites with you! Thanks, -Holcomb Crew | | | | | | Paid for by Eric Holcomb for Indiana. © Copyright 2020. subject: One thing
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota
body:
| [](https://action.tinaforminnesota.?g=YCjTcDpTVzRarKyUt8_RZpA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tsm-em200219-amy?refcode=em200219-amy&recurring=yes&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amount=10") Team --

The presidential candidates will return to the debate stage tonight and discuss the pressing issues facing our country. But there's one thing that's on all of our minds:

**It won't be enough to win the White House if we don't win the Senate.**

My good friend Amy Klobuchar knows it. I know it. And I'm sure you do, too. This task is much bigger than one campaign alone -- it's about all of us coming together to defeat Donald Trump and end Mitch McConnell's stranglehold on power.

That's why Amy and I are working together to make sure Democrats win, and we're asking:

**Will you split a donation between our campaigns today? Anything you can give will go a long way toward supporting our campaign for U.S. Senate and Amy's campaign for president, and will bring Democrats one step closer to getting Washington working for the people again.** _If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [$10 »](https://action.tinaforminnesota.?g=YCjTcDpTVzRarKyUt8_RZpA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tsm-em200219-amy?refcode=em200219-amy&recurring=yes&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=&express_lane=true&amount=10")

Protecting our health care. Defending reproductive freedom. Tackling the climate crisis. Raising wages for working families. If we want to make progress on all of these issues and more, Democrats must win up and down the ballot.

So Amy and I are teaming up, rolling up our sleeves, and working to make sure Democrats win big in November. **[Will you join us today?](https://action.tinaforminnesota.?g=YCjTcDpTVzRarKyUt8_RZpA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tsm-em200219-amy?refcode=em200219-amy&recurring=yes&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")**

Thanks for everything.

-- Tina

name: Cory Booker
subject: The Trump budget.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cory Booker for Senate
body:
| | |
---
| Trump wants to cut Medicaid, food stamps, and more. Team, have you seen the Trump administration's latest budget proposal? Massive funding cuts are being proposed to Medicaid, food stamps, affordable housing, education, student loan assistance, environmental protection, and so much more. These federal programs are lifelines for American families. The consequences of these funding cuts would be absolutely devastating, especially for communities like mine in Newark. **[Please stand with me in fighting back against the Trump budget. Add your name if you agree that these funding cuts absolutely cannot be passed into law.](https://act.corybooker.%3D/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://act.corybooker.com/page/s/oppose-the-trump-budget?source=em_pet_20200219_CB_20200219-pet-CB-EM1-actives_actives_l1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")** It would be unconscionable to scale back these federal programs at a time when millions of Americans depend upon them. No family should have to worry about their food stamps or access to health care being cut as a result of the cruelty of this administration. We can't let this happen. Now that you know where I stand, I encourage you to contact your other elected officials in Washington and urge them to oppose the Trump budget as well. **[Sign your name here if you'll join me in fighting back against this heartless budget proposal. We won't stand by as this President inflicts more pain on the most vulnerable Americans.](https://act.corybooker.%3D/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://act.corybooker.com/page/s/oppose-the-trump-budget?source=em_pet_20200219_CB_20200219-pet-CB-EM1-actives_actives_l2&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")** **Donald Trump may want to try cutting funding for health care, food stamps, and other important programs -- but it 's not going to happen on my watch.** It's time to make our voices heard. Cory [](https://act.corybooker.%3D/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://act.corybooker.com/page/s/oppose-the-trump-budget?source=em_pet_20200219_CB_20200219-pet-CB-EM1-actives_actives_button&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")

---

If you want the most up-to-date information, follow Cory on [Instagram](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0fa7/5fca0d19/7a0f3d3e/291d09d7/1617615692/VEsC/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://www.instagram.com/corybooker/"), [Twitter](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0fa7/5fca0d19/7a0f3d3e/291d09d6/1617615692/VEsD/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://twitter.com/corybooker"), and [Facebook](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0fa7/5fca0d19/7a0f3d3e/291d09d5/1617615692/VEsA/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://www.facebook.com/corybooker").

name: Heather Hargreaves
subject: A letter to Tom2020 staff and supporters
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TOM STEYER 2020]()
body:
| | | |
---
Hey — I wanted to share this internal memo that I’ve sent to our Tom2020 staff and endorsers ahead of the Nevada caucuses. It’s a lot of information, but I’m really proud of everything we’ve built, so I hope you’ll read through. This race is still wide open, and we’re seeing incredible energy from supporters in both Nevada and South Carolina! Below, you’ll find important updates from me about the state of the race overall, the DNC debate criteria, our formidable ground game in Nevada and South Carolina, our standout online organizing, the campaign’s plans for Super Tuesday, and of course, what you can do to help Tom as the campaign moves forward. Take a look and let me know if you have thoughts or questions. I love hearing from all of you who are on this team and by Tom’s side. Thank you. HH
---
|
---
TO: Tom2020 Team (Staff, Donors, Endorsers, and Supporters) FROM: Heather Hargreaves, Campaign Manager, Tom2020 DATE: February 19, 2020 RE: STATE OF THE RACE
---
|
---
**SUMMARY** Recent polling makes it clear that Tom is well-positioned in Nevada and South Carolina and, with the right message, will break out of the pack with significant momentum heading into Super Tuesday. Tom’s absence from tonight’s debate stage is indicative only of the short-sighted qualification policies set by the DNC and not an indicator of how Tom is performing in either state. The DNC rules arbitrarily admit some polls while excluding others. Every public poll during this debate window, and our internal polls, have shown that Tom is clearly above the threshold to be in the debate and is in line to have a strong showing this weekend in Nevada. Within the last month, Nevada and South Carolina have seen six public polls where Tom is at or above 10% in every one, and over 12% in three. While we would like to be in the debate, debates are not elections. The facts are:
* This race is wide open as no candidate has come to define the race. These next two states are critical tests for all the candidates in the race.
* Tom is surging at the right time. As the race moves into more diverse states that better reflect the demographics of the party, Tom is polling better and his message is resonating.
* Tom is a true progressive Democrat. He is building the broad coalition needed to defeat Donald Trump in the fall. He is pulling together people from red districts, African Americans, Latinos, and non-traditional voters. These are the key voters who will provide the support needed to dump Donald Trump.
* He is uniquely electable. Tom is the only one with the experience necessary to beat Donald Trump on the economy. He has demonstrated the ability to build a diverse coalition. And as a Washington outsider, he will be able to break the stranglehold that the wealthy and corporations have over our nation’s agenda.
* We are seeing a steady surge of energy on the ground. At every stop in the last few weeks, we have seen more and more people come out, more media attention and more endorsements from community leaders who really matter and will help Tom get elected.
* Tom’s family is all in. They are criss-crossing the country and going to every corner of Nevada and South Carolina to talk about how Tom is the best candidate for the presidency.
* Our ground game is solid. We have one of the strongest organizing teams in Nevada and South Carolina. Unlike other campaigns, Tom believes that if you want to develop support in a community, then you need to hire from that community and then listen when they talk. Tom’s team is knocking on doors, making phone calls and developing the connections necessary to drive turnout and win the election.
* We have standout organizing online. We aren’t building an online ATM like other candidates. We are focused on building meaningful connections that encourages people to share our message to their networks. We have had millions of engagements and continue to refine our approach through rigorous testing.
* The Super Tuesday strategy is beginning to see real results. Our hybrid model merging field and political outreach is garnering endorsements and earned media that is getting Tom’s message out to the voters just as he will have momentum coming out of the early states.

**STATE OF THE RACE** Regardless of the turnout model, Tom earns well over the 12% support threshold put forth by the DNC to make the Nevada debate. Let me be clear; if it were up to the people of Nevada and South Carolina, Tom Steyer would be on the debate stage tonight. Unfortunately, the DNC has rules for this debate that do not allow for any early state polling except one poll in Nevada to count towards the debate qualifying criteria. Despite the DNC rules, we know that Tom is doing well in Nevada and South Carolina. In the last month there have been six public polls in those states. Tom has been at or above 10% in every one and over 12% in three of them. And Tom’s current polling average on Real Clear Politics is 10.5% in Nevada and 16% in South Carolina, putting him in the top tier of candidates in both states. One week before the DNC started its polling window for qualification to the Nevada debate, our campaign met the polling criteria in two Fox News polls that showed his campaign at 15% in South Carolina and 12% in Nevada. More recent polls conducted by The Post and Courier and East Carolina University confirmed Tom’s momentum, showing him as a leading candidate with 18% and 19% support respectively. The Post and Courier poll showed Tom earning 24% support amongst black voters, only six points behind first-placed Joe Biden. While it is certainly frustrating and disappointing that Tom will not be on the stage tonight, we know that Tom is in a good position in the next two states and has the message and organization to compete well beyond that. **THIS RACE IS WIDE OPEN AND TOM IS SURGING**
What is clear after Iowa and New Hampshire is that the race to the Democratic nomination is wide open. With only two small, racially homogeneous states voting there is no clear front runner. Until Super Tuesday, when a plurality of delegates are decided, we can’t define this race. A new poll conducted by Change Research, has Tom at 20% and in second in SC, trailing Vice President Biden by only 3% as Biden’s numbers continue to worsen. Another poll done by Beacon Research for our campaign shows Tom statistically tied with Biden for 2nd place, and trailing Senator Sanders by only 6 points in Nevada. The numbers reveal Tom’s strength with African Americans and Latinos, two voting blocks that are critical to picking up delegates in Nevada, South Carolina, and the delegate-rich Super Tuesday states.
**TOM APPEALS TO DIVERSE AMERICA WHO WILL WIN US THIS ELECTION**
Tom has spent a decade building broad and diverse coalitions to beat corporations and win important victories for America. He has worked extensively with black and brown communities, young people, voters focused on climate, rural communities that are often plagued by pollution, and non-traditional voters. Tom’s core audience reflects the true colorful tapestry that is America. And his numbers are growing in these communities, which are certain to determine the General Election outcome. Recent polls conducted by The Post and Courier and East Carolina University confirmed Tom’s momentum in South Carolina, showing him as a leading candidate with 18% and 19% support. The Post and Courier poll showed Tom earning 24% support amongst black voters in South Carolina, only six points behind first-placed Joe Biden, who may be taking the black vote for granted. We are earning it!
**TOM UNIQUELY HAS “ELECTABILITY”**
We know that Tom’s profile as a candidate is unique. He is the only person left in the race who has never held public office. He didn’t enter the race as a well-known former candidate, vice president, or mayor. But Tom’s business background makes him the strongest candidate to be able to go head-to-head with Donald Trump on the economy and win. We know that politics-as-usual isn’t going to beat Trump. As more and more Democrats get to know Tom, especially after the Nevada Caucus, the South Carolina debate and its subsequent primary, voters will see he is the best person to beat Trump. **TOM2020 ENERGY ON THE GROUND** Tom is seeing a steady surge of energy on the ground in both Nevada and South Carolina where he has spent the majority of his time even since Iowa. Some of the high turnout events and important endorsements included: in Nevada —
* A 1,000 person rally in Las Vegas on Feb. 5.
* A block party in Winnsboro, SC with over 300 people attending and State Representative Annie McDaniel endorsing.
* A meet and greet with over 200 people in Gardnerville, just outside of Reno NV, where Tom was introduced by Jill Derby, former Chairwoman of the Nevada Democratic Party
* A meet and greet in Nevada’s capital, Carson City with a crowd of over 200 people at the campaign’s Carson office.

And in South Carolina —
* An endorsement by Spartanburg Councilman Michael Brown.
* An armchair conversation with former State Representative Harold Mitchell and Dr. Charles Steele of the historic Southern Christian Leadership Council at a standing-room only event.
* A town hall in Greenville with over 500 people attending, featuring an introduction from endorser State Representative Chandra Dillard.

**TOM’S FAMILY IS ALL IN**
Tom’s family is out on the campaign trail spreading Tom’s message - particularly to African American and Latino audiences, critical endorsers and grass-tops leaders. Sam and Evi Steyer have been crisscrossing Nevada doing campaign events in Minden, Las Vegas, and one in Sparks where they delivered their remarks fully in Spanish. They also have been critical in helping the field team recruit precinct captains.
Kat Taylor, Tom’s wife, moved to South Carolina symbolizing and capitalizing on the fact that as South Carolina goes, so go the Super Tuesday southern states. She has focused particularly on hosting a series of gatherings of African American women, Latinas and AAPI constituents to discuss economic justice and solicit their ideas. Just yesterday, Kat carried over that conversation with AAPI folk and black women in Las Vegas NV. So far, these events have reached at least 500 people, each with concentric circles of influence in their communities.
**GROUND GAME IN NV AND SC IS STRONG AND BUILDING**
The Tom 2020 campaign has some of the biggest and best field operations on the ground in Nevada and South Carolina. Led by a strong team of women, our Nevada program is focused on organizing in the communities across Nevada where they are from. With a team of more than 50 organizers, the campaign is focused on building a strong precinct captain program and doing events that support the diverse residents of Nevada. This week the team in Nevada has secured some critical endorsements including tribal leaders Arlan and Joyce Melendez, who join State Senator Marcia Washington, Berna Ford and many others in supporting Tom.
In South Carolina, we have 53 organizers who are primarily from the state and organizing in their own communities. Almost half of our staff is working within 10 miles of where they were born. Our team is not just organizing in traditional areas of Columbia and Charleston. They are working in the often neglected areas of the state like Denmark, Hampton, Chester, Bennetsville, and Hopkins - rural communities that are food deserts, health care deserts, and areas facing a water crisis. Tom’s support in South Carolina runs deep with 12 members of the state legislature supporting him. Importantly, Representative Gilda Cobb Hunter, who is the longest standing African American female legislator and who has never before endorsed a presidential candidate, has become a senior advisor to the campaign where her wisdom and insights are producing enormous results. And the list is growing every day. **STANDOUT ORGANIZING ONLINE** We aren’t just seeing support grow on the ground, we also are seeing it online. Our team has excelled in developing and supporting tools for our supporters to organize online. Our TeamTom app supports relational organizing and content sharing, allowing all activists to share content and track commitment and activity from their friends and family. And we are engaging supporters over Facebook messenger, including over 2 million conversations so far. Our online team also helps people engage offline across the country. We have over 4500 volunteers who have sent handwritten notecards to supporters and undecided voters in Nevada and South Carolina. TeamTom is running one of the most sophisticated, data-driven digital advertising programs in the country. We're testing and running hundreds of different pieces of creative at any given time, all the way from broad focus-group-tested persuasion messages to micro-targeted local endorsement messages. Thanks to the constant testing since launch, we're able to run our best-of-the-best, most effective content to voters in Nevada and South Carolina this month. This program is ready and able to compete with Donald Trump's massive misinformation machine, both from an investment perspective and a strategy perspective. In addition to our expansive digital advertising program, we have built an organic content infrastructure that's different from any other political candidate's program. Opting against treating our considerably sized email list as an ATM, making demands in a one-way conversation, we have instead built a communication apparatus that gives long-time, newly-joined, and prospective supporters meaningful information about who Tom is and what his policies would be as president. We've established a dialogue with our supporters via ongoing surveys, customized information by state and interest, and maintained a heavy focus on voter education for all. This kind of educational, persuasive email program is made possible only by forgoing the traditional model of constant "triple match," "final notice," false urgency fundraising. **THE SUPER TUESDAY STRATEGY IS BEGINNING TO SEE REAL RESULTS** As our campaign builds capacity towards Super Tuesday, we have focused on community lead organizing and outreach. Tom is committed to building a diverse coalition to take on Donald Trump and this is clear in Super Tuesday states where our leadership and organizers have made building this coalition a priority.

This is reflected in some of our endorsements in Super Tuesday states, including:
* Alabama Civil Rights Activist Reverend Gwendolyn Cook Webb who was a leader in the historic 1963 Birmingham Children’s Marches. Cook’s pioneering story continued as she later became the second-ever African-American female police officer hired by the Birmingham police department.
* Angela Bridgeman, North Carolina Democratic Party Precinct Chair and a transgender woman who is running for state office in North Carolina, a state that has systemically tried to strip the rights of LGBTQ people. Running as an outsider, Bridgeman endorsed the only outsider candidate left in the race.
* Former CA State Senator Fran Pavley. Pavley served two terms in the California State Senate and three terms in the California State Assembly, chairing the Senate Natural Resources and Water Committee and earning widespread renown for her pioneering work on environmental and climate change legislation.
* CA Assemblymember Eduardo Garcia - Garcia is the current chair of the Committee on Water, Parks and Wildlife. Garcia most recently led the way on AB 617, a first-of-its-kind program that protects communities disproportionately exposed to air pollution, and SB 200, which funds access to clean drinking water to communities who lack it.

**WHAT CAN YOU DO?** Keep doing what you are doing! Keep organizing and spreading the word about Tom and his message. This fight is only just beginning and it's going to take focus and dedication to see it through. Don’t forget to:
* Make calls into Nevada and South Carolina! Every day our team is talking to voters and you can from anywhere. [_**Go to www.tomsteyer.com to sign up.**_](http://clicks.tomsteyer.com/ls/click?upn=M5l2K6CF-2BTeSi2jZtEX6WBDyNJv5vZjyKQ4j7cPI74seIcpG7UhnTkIwtZ-2BM5x1gu2RHqQeHrPXLGYnj5yM636-2BiB3wkfasSRnbM4v9JtHudCuzYLZMoWFq5-2BrBgkkXdO8dzw-2B7-2Ffsx-2B6Z8j-2BwyBw5Le1BsRccKa-2B-2BA8H2i5HlsXMD-2FXuiDX13rEBsKsPsQ55lg3yEsCAsJ6-2BZCVPd2Dtg-3D-3Dv2Dr_XwU9lPq5-2Bze3gI64ohlV0cQwWAuBAU7ADIvj2CS7ymjl4gIclaYtJbufimVIRBDNvK8HU03xUgW0NhkSEhAU84K-2ByBrP4G-2Big5L1tMaaDmwYsvXY5MjlGhfQh0D9ppVbLIexEJXhgI-2F2n0VXNCJHMM3sMeD8kvfJSPDrON3uqrmr7iegvL9qGhWeW1od86NHtFXRK4jh2dtILB8ZugeKB-2Bx9RyijjooI5Fz65apK8nML8hIrXKVG7UzCntuCyVRwwCRR6Nkn6bnRFHdLMnCtazkRQVy31YZsWSDpqmTllViuFOD18kNnj-2BtMZDbrpLIUTiAoZ9ZsqQCiWznmt62CPJUlGobhcW-2F6lnZsSgD7sK9wZtbeQFfPJeCPSysZvcT1WV3by35nE7vcAFq5BwRwOX1Z8yrD-2B0LWIJZVjQ5sNqIzfk3i-2B-2BFmnaaeF-2FN1Yv0xhW6BgZw9SkU962wBo53uhCU7QqA-2BbikYZu9PdZZpiUGGJl1QT7NmJIGAx4OyERgyt8txFrcCXYXvaQa-2F8O48nqwTFg3HoYPQuDgJmVCEpA8-3D)

clean_body: | | | | Hey — I wanted to share this internal memo that I’ve sent to our Tom2020 staff and endorsers ahead of the Nevada caucuses. It’s a lot of information, but I’m really proud of everything we’ve built, so I hope you’ll read through. This race is still wide open, and we’re seeing incredible energy from supporters in both Nevada and South Carolina! Below, you’ll find important updates from me about the state of the race overall, the DNC debate criteria, our formidable ground game in Nevada and South Carolina, our standout online organizing, the campaign’s plans for Super Tuesday, and of course, what you can do to help Tom as the campaign moves forward. Take a look and let me know if you have thoughts or questions. I love hearing from all of you who are on this team and by Tom’s side. Thank you. HH | TO: Tom2020 Team (Staff, Donors, Endorsers, and Supporters) FROM: Heather Hargreaves, Campaign Manager, Tom2020 DATE: February 19, 2020 RE: STATE OF THE RACE | SUMMARY Recent polling makes it clear that Tom is well-positioned in Nevada and South Carolina and, with the right message, will break out of the pack with significant momentum heading into Super Tuesday. Tom’s absence from tonight’s debate stage is indicative only of the short-sighted qualification policies set by the DNC and not an indicator of how Tom is performing in either state. The DNC rules arbitrarily admit some polls while excluding others. Every public poll during this debate window, and our internal polls, have shown that Tom is clearly above the threshold to be in the debate and is in line to have a strong showing this weekend in Nevada. Within the last month, Nevada and South Carolina have seen six public polls where Tom is at or above 10% in every one, and over 12% in three. While we would like to be in the debate, debates are not elections. The facts are: This race is wide open as no candidate has come to define the race. These next two states are critical tests for all the candidates in the race. Tom is surging at the right time. As the race moves into more diverse states that better reflect the demographics of the party, Tom is polling better and his message is resonating. Tom is a true progressive Democrat. He is building the broad coalition needed to defeat Donald Trump in the fall. He is pulling together people from red districts, African Americans, Latinos, and non-traditional voters. These are the key voters who will provide the support needed to dump Donald Trump. He is uniquely electable. Tom is the only one with the experience necessary to beat Donald Trump on the economy. He has demonstrated the ability to build a diverse coalition. And as a Washington outsider, he will be able to break the stranglehold that the wealthy and corporations have over our nation’s agenda. We are seeing a steady surge of energy on the ground. At every stop in the last few weeks, we have seen more and more people come out, more media attention and more endorsements from community leaders who really matter and will help Tom get elected. Tom’s family is all in. They are criss-crossing the country and going to every corner of Nevada and South Carolina to talk about how Tom is the best candidate for the presidency. Our ground game is solid. We have one of the strongest organizing teams in Nevada and South Carolina. Unlike other campaigns, Tom believes that if you want to develop support in a community, then you need to hire from that community and then listen when they talk. Tom’s team is knocking on doors, making phone calls and developing the connections necessary to drive turnout and win the election. We have standout organizing online. We aren’t building an online ATM like other candidates. We are focused on building meaningful connections that encourages people to share our message to their networks. We have had millions of engagements and continue to refine our approach through rigorous testing. The Super Tuesday strategy is beginning to see real results. Our hybrid model merging field and political outreach is garnering endorsements and earned media that is getting Tom’s message out to the voters just as he will have momentum coming out of the early states. STATE OF THE RACE Regardless of the turnout model, Tom earns well over the 12% support threshold put forth by the DNC to make the Nevada debate. Let me be clear; if it were up to the people of Nevada and South Carolina, Tom Steyer would be on the debate stage tonight. Unfortunately, the DNC has rules for this debate that do not allow for any early state polling except one poll in Nevada to count towards the debate qualifying criteria. Despite the DNC rules, we know that Tom is doing well in Nevada and South Carolina. In the last month there have been six public polls in those states. Tom has been at or above 10% in every one and over 12% in three of them. And Tom’s current polling average on Real Clear Politics is 10.5% in Nevada and 16% in South Carolina, putting him in the top tier of candidates in both states. One week before the DNC started its polling window for qualification to the Nevada debate, our campaign met the polling criteria in two Fox News polls that showed his campaign at 15% in South Carolina and 12% in Nevada. More recent polls conducted by The Post and Courier and East Carolina University confirmed Tom’s momentum, showing him as a leading candidate with 18% and 19% support respectively. The Post and Courier poll showed Tom earning 24% support amongst black voters, only six points behind first-placed Joe Biden. While it is certainly frustrating and disappointing that Tom will not be on the stage tonight, we know that Tom is in a good position in the next two states and has the message and organization to compete well beyond that. THIS RACE IS WIDE OPEN AND TOM IS SURGING What is clear after Iowa and New Hampshire is that the race to the Democratic nomination is wide open. With only two small, racially homogeneous states voting there is no clear front runner. Until Super Tuesday, when a plurality of delegates are decided, we can’t define this race. A new poll conducted by Change Research, has Tom at 20% and in second in SC, trailing Vice President Biden by only 3% as Biden’s numbers continue to worsen. Another poll done by Beacon Research for our campaign shows Tom statistically tied with Biden for 2nd place, and trailing Senator Sanders by only 6 points in Nevada. The numbers reveal Tom’s strength with African Americans and Latinos, two voting blocks that are critical to picking up delegates in Nevada, South Carolina, and the delegate-rich Super Tuesday states. TOM APPEALS TO DIVERSE AMERICA WHO WILL WIN US THIS ELECTION Tom has spent a decade building broad and diverse coalitions to beat corporations and win important victories for America. He has worked extensively with black and brown communities, young people, voters focused on climate, rural communities that are often plagued by pollution, and non-traditional voters. Tom’s core audience reflects the true colorful tapestry that is America. And his numbers are growing in these communities, which are certain to determine the General Election outcome. Recent polls conducted by The Post and Courier and East Carolina University confirmed Tom’s momentum in South Carolina, showing him as a leading candidate with 18% and 19% support. The Post and Courier poll showed Tom earning 24% support amongst black voters in South Carolina, only six points behind first-placed Joe Biden, who may be taking the black vote for granted. We are earning it! TOM UNIQUELY HAS “ELECTABILITY” We know that Tom’s profile as a candidate is unique. He is the only person left in the race who has never held public office. He didn’t enter the race as a well-known former candidate, vice president, or mayor. But Tom’s business background makes him the strongest candidate to be able to go head-to-head with Donald Trump on the economy and win. We know that politics-as-usual isn’t going to beat Trump. As more and more Democrats get to know Tom, especially after the Nevada Caucus, the South Carolina debate and its subsequent primary, voters will see he is the best person to beat Trump. TOM2020 ENERGY ON THE GROUND Tom is seeing a steady surge of energy on the ground in both Nevada and South Carolina where he has spent the majority of his time even since Iowa. Some of the high turnout events and important endorsements included: in Nevada — A 1,000 person rally in Las Vegas on Feb. 5. A block party in Winnsboro, SC with over 300 people attending and State Representative Annie McDaniel endorsing. A meet and greet with over 200 people in Gardnerville, just outside of Reno NV, where Tom was introduced by Jill Derby, former Chairwoman of the Nevada Democratic Party A meet and greet in Nevada’s capital, Carson City with a crowd of over 200 people at the campaign’s Carson office. And in South Carolina — An endorsement by Spartanburg Councilman Michael Brown. An armchair conversation with former State Representative Harold Mitchell and Dr. Charles Steele of the historic Southern Christian Leadership Council at a standing-room only event. A town hall in Greenville with over 500 people attending, featuring an introduction from endorser State Representative Chandra Dillard. TOM’S FAMILY IS ALL IN Tom’s family is out on the campaign trail spreading Tom’s message - particularly to African American and Latino audiences, critical endorsers and grass-tops leaders. Sam and Evi Steyer have been crisscrossing Nevada doing campaign events in Minden, Las Vegas, and one in Sparks where they delivered their remarks fully in Spanish. They also have been critical in helping the field team recruit precinct captains. Kat Taylor, Tom’s wife, moved to South Carolina symbolizing and capitalizing on the fact that as South Carolina goes, so go the Super Tuesday southern states. She has focused particularly on hosting a series of gatherings of African American women, Latinas and AAPI constituents to discuss economic justice and solicit their ideas. Just yesterday, Kat carried over that conversation with AAPI folk and black women in Las Vegas NV. So far, these events have reached at least 500 people, each with concentric circles of influence in their communities. GROUND GAME IN NV AND SC IS STRONG AND BUILDING The Tom 2020 campaign has some of the biggest and best field operations on the ground in Nevada and South Carolina. Led by a strong team of women, our Nevada program is focused on organizing in the communities across Nevada where they are from. With a team of more than 50 organizers, the campaign is focused on building a strong precinct captain program and doing events that support the diverse residents of Nevada. This week the team in Nevada has secured some critical endorsements including tribal leaders Arlan and Joyce Melendez, who join State Senator Marcia Washington, Berna Ford and many others in supporting Tom. In South Carolina, we have 53 organizers who are primarily from the state and organizing in their own communities. Almost half of our staff is working within 10 miles of where they were born. Our team is not just organizing in traditional areas of Columbia and Charleston. They are working in the often neglected areas of the state like Denmark, Hampton, Chester, Bennetsville, and Hopkins - rural communities that are food deserts, health care deserts, and areas facing a water crisis. Tom’s support in South Carolina runs deep with 12 members of the state legislature supporting him. Importantly, Representative Gilda Cobb Hunter, who is the longest standing African American female legislator and who has never before endorsed a presidential candidate, has become a senior advisor to the campaign where her wisdom and insights are producing enormous results. And the list is growing every day. STANDOUT ORGANIZING ONLINE We aren’t just seeing support grow on the ground, we also are seeing it online. Our team has excelled in developing and supporting tools for our supporters to organize online. Our TeamTom app supports relational organizing and content sharing, allowing all activists to share content and track commitment and activity from their friends and family. And we are engaging supporters over Facebook messenger, including over 2 million conversations so far. Our online team also helps people engage offline across the country. We have over 4500 volunteers who have sent handwritten notecards to supporters and undecided voters in Nevada and South Carolina. TeamTom is running one of the most sophisticated, data-driven digital advertising programs in the country. We're testing and running hundreds of different pieces of creative at any given time, all the way from broad focus-group-tested persuasion messages to micro-targeted local endorsement messages. Thanks to the constant testing since launch, we're able to run our best-of-the-best, most effective content to voters in Nevada and South Carolina this month. This program is ready and able to compete with Donald Trump's massive misinformation machine, both from an investment perspective and a strategy perspective. In addition to our expansive digital advertising program, we have built an organic content infrastructure that's different from any other political candidate's program. Opting against treating our considerably sized email list as an ATM, making demands in a one-way conversation, we have instead built a communication apparatus that gives long-time, newly-joined, and prospective supporters meaningful information about who Tom is and what his policies would be as president. We've established a dialogue with our supporters via ongoing surveys, customized information by state and interest, and maintained a heavy focus on voter education for all. This kind of educational, persuasive email program is made possible only by forgoing the traditional model of constant "triple match," "final notice," false urgency fundraising. THE SUPER TUESDAY STRATEGY IS BEGINNING TO SEE REAL RESULTS As our campaign builds capacity towards Super Tuesday, we have focused on community lead organizing and outreach. Tom is committed to building a diverse coalition to take on Donald Trump and this is clear in Super Tuesday states where our leadership and organizers have made building this coalition a priority. This is reflected in some of our endorsements in Super Tuesday states, including: Alabama Civil Rights Activist Reverend Gwendolyn Cook Webb who was a leader in the historic 1963 Birmingham Children’s Marches. Cook’s pioneering story continued as she later became the second-ever African-American female police officer hired by the Birmingham police department. Angela Bridgeman, North Carolina Democratic Party Precinct Chair and a transgender woman who is running for state office in North Carolina, a state that has systemically tried to strip the rights of LGBTQ people. Running as an outsider, Bridgeman endorsed the only outsider candidate left in the race. Former CA State Senator Fran Pavley. Pavley served two terms in the California State Senate and three terms in the California State Assembly, chairing the Senate Natural Resources and Water Committee and earning widespread renown for her pioneering work on environmental and climate change legislation. CA Assemblymember Eduardo Garcia - Garcia is the current chair of the Committee on Water, Parks and Wildlife. Garcia most recently led the way on AB 617, a first-of-its-kind program that protects communities disproportionately exposed to air pollution, and SB 200, which funds access to clean drinking water to communities who lack it. WHAT CAN YOU DO? Keep doing what you are doing! Keep organizing and spreading the word about Tom and his message. This fight is only just beginning and it's going to take focus and dedication to see it through. Don’t forget to: Make calls into Nevada and South Carolina! Every day our team is talking to voters and you can from anywhere. to share information with friends and family. name: Steve Chabot
subject: A Bloomberg - Hillary Ticket?
body:
Is this really happening? No images? [Click here](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-e-jhtybjl-kujkulhluu-z/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhtybjl-kujkulhluu-r/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhtybjl-kujkulhluu-y/)
### [A Bloomberg - Hillary Ticket?](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhtybjl-kujkulhluu-j/)
I first heard the story over the weekend that Mike Bloomberg might be considering Hillary Clinton as a vice presidential running mate. Like me, maybe this caught you by surprise? In this week's blog I write about how likely we could see a Bloomberg - Hillary Democrat ticket. [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhtybjl-kujkulhluu-t/)
# Previous Blog Posts
[](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhtybjl-kujkulhluu-i/)
### [Fentanyl is a Killer](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhtybjl-kujkulhluu-d/)
The good news is drug overdose deaths in the United States are down over the last year. The bad news is that there are still far too many. In this week's blog, I discuss what Congress is doing to help fight the opioid epidemic, specifically targeting fentanyl. [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhtybjl-kujkulhluu-h/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhtybjl-kujkulhluu-k/)
### [Four Days in February](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhtybjl-kujkulhluu-u/)
Super Bowl Sunday, confusion in Iowa on Monday, State of the Union on Tuesday and today, Wednesday, President Trump will be acquitted! In this week's blog, I discuss this week's more interesting moments. [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhtybjl-kujkulhluu-o/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhtybjl-kujkulhluu-b/)
### [Hundreds of Thousands of Marchers - Ignored Again](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhtybjl-kujkulhluu-n/)
Last Friday, hundreds of thousands of people of all ages gathered in Washington, D.C. for the 2020 March for Life. It was an inspiring event and in fact, one of my favorite events of the year. In this week's blog, I discuss the march and make the case for why this event should no longer be ignored by the mainstream press. Little lives do matter! [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhtybjl-kujkulhluu-p/) [Donate](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhtybjl-kujkulhluu-x/) [Get a Yard Sign](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhtybjl-kujkulhluu-m/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhtybjl-kujkulhluu-c/) | [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhtybjl-kujkulhluu-q/)| [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhtybjl-kujkulhluu-a/)| [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhtybjl-kujkulhluu-f/)
---|---|---
6613 Glenway Ave, Cincinnati, OH 45211

name: Steve Chabot
subject: ICYMI: Rep. Steve Chabot: Permanently ban fentanyl-related drugs
body:
I promise this will be fast and won't take up much of your time, but as January wraps up, we have a big goal we need to meet. If we don't meet this goal, it could indicate to the Democrats that they have the upper hand. No images? [Click here](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-e-jhtydluy-kujkulhluu-i/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhtydluy-kujkulhluu-r/) **ICYMI: Rep. Steve Chabot: Permanently ban fentanyl-related drugs** _Cincinnati Enquirer_ February 19, 2020 In recent years, opioid addiction has claimed tens of thousands of American lives and shattered families across our nation. That's why we must work together to redouble our efforts to combat this deadly epidemic.Opioids, when used properly, can be an effective medical tool for pain relief. However, when opioids are abused, they're extremely dangerous. While fatalities from prescription opioids have been declining over the last few years, fatalities from other forms of opioids, such as fentanyl, have been increasing at an alarming rate.In searching for answers to this plague, I've worked with local and national drug experts. I've also worked with local law enforcement, medical personnel and community leaders and discussed with them the tools they need to effectively fight this battle. And as a member of the Bipartisan Opioid Task Force, I've worked closely with my colleagues from across the country focused on finding legislative solutions to this ongoing problem....Recently, there's been a massive influx into the country of cheap but incredibly powerful opioids, such as fentanyl, from China and other foreign sources. A lot of these illegal drugs enter via our southern border, so securing that border is critical.Giving law enforcement the tools they need to combat these dangerous drugs is just as important. A drug as deadly as fentanyl has no place in our communities. It's at least 50 times more potent than heroin, and ingesting even a minuscule amount can be fatal....That's why I introduced legislation, H.R. 5771, the FIGHT Fentanyl Act, along with fellow Ohio Congressman Bob Latta. Companion legislation was introduced in the Senate by our Senator Rob Portman and West Virginia's Senator Joe Manchin. Our legislation would permanently criminalize the manufacture, distribution or possession of fentanyl analogs....Congress doesn't need to spend more time deciding if fentanyl analogs are dangerous. We know they're more than just dangerous, they're deadly, and should be kept off our streets and out of our neighborhoods. Now is the time to make the criminalization of fentanyl analogs permanent. Too many lives and families have been ruined by the scourge of this deadly drug for Congress to continue passing temporary, incomplete measures.**[Read the full op-ed in the Cincinnati Enquirer here.](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhtydluy-kujkulhluu-y/)** | [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhtydluy-kujkulhluu-j/)| [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jhtydluy-kujkulhluu-t/)
---|---
6613 Glenway Ave, Cincinnati, OH 45211

name: Marsha Blackburn
subject: The White House is not for sale
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Blackburn Tennessee Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee
body:
President Trump knows that the White House and our Republican Senate Majority cannot be bought. He knows that it takes effective leadership, strategic policies and a dedication to getting to know and understand the Americans you're fighting for.

If you would prefer not to receive future emails from Blackburn Tennessee Victory Fund, click [here.](http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=NDE4Mjc4NjYS1&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTcwMTY4MTI1MwS2&mt=1&rt=0)
****
****
__

Peter,

The Democrats were on stage tonight, once again slinging mud at one another over their radical, liberal ideas, and their desperation is becoming painfully obvious.

Bernie Sanders is now leading Democrats in most national polls, showcasing how truly fractured and radical the Left is, to start coalescing around a socialist. And if that wasn't enough, tonight, the candidates were joined by someone Tennesseans know all too well: Billionaire Mike Bloomberg.

For those of you who don't remember, liberal Mike Bloomberg bankrolled my opponent's campaign in 2018 and tried to buy Tennessee's Senate seat, so it should come as no surprise that he's trying to buy the presidency now.

**Peter, President Trump knows that the White House and our Republican Senate Majority cannot be bought.**

**He knows that it takes effective leadership, strategic policies and a dedication to getting to know and understand the Americans you 're fighting for.**

That's why he's traveling across the country, rallying hardworking individuals and families in every corner of our great nation, from Colorado to Nevada to Iowa to New Hampshire to Arizona and beyond! And that's why President Trump has seen record Republican turnout in the Iowa Caucuses and New Hampshire primary, and it's why we anticipate conservatives around the country to continue to demonstrate their support for this Administration all the way through Election Day in November.

**Help us keep up the momentum by making a contribution to help us defend the Republican Senate Majority that has confirmed over 180 of President Trump 's judicial nominees, delivered historic tax cuts for all Americans and worked tirelessly to advance President Trump's agenda!**

Donate using this secure link: [secure.winred.com/btvf/stand-with-potus](http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?kn=3&ms=NDE4Mjc4NjYS1&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTcwMTY4MTI1MwS2&mt=1&rt=0)

The Democrats will continue to drag out their infighting and indecisiveness on just how far to the left they're willing to drag the party in order to regain power.

Meanwhile, President Trump and the Republicans will continue to roll up their sleeves and get to work for the American people, and continue to deliver on promises made to boost our economy, get the country back to work and Make America Great Again!

Thank you in advance for your commitment to the conservative cause, Peter. Your ongoing support is invaluable.

Let's keep up the fight,

Marsha Blackburn
U.S. Senator (R-TN)

_****_
**[**DONATE**](http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?kn=6&ms=NDE4Mjc4NjYS1&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTcwMTY4MTI1MwS2&mt=1&rt=0)**
---

Bernie Sanders is now leading Democrats in most national polls, showcasing how truly fractured and radical the Left is, to start coalescing around a socialist. And if that wasn't enough, tonight, the candidates were joined by someone Tennesseans know all too well: Billionaire Mike Bloomberg. For those of you who don't remember, liberal Mike Bloomberg bankrolled my opponent's campaign in 2018 and tried to buy Tennessee's Senate seat, so it should come as no surprise that he's trying to buy the presidency now. Peter, President Trump knows that the White House and our Republican Senate Majority cannot be bought. He knows that it takes effective leadership, strategic policies and a dedication to getting to know and understand the Americans you 're fighting for. That's why he's traveling across the country, rallying hardworking individuals and families in every corner of our great nation, from Colorado to Nevada to Iowa to New Hampshire to Arizona and beyond! And that's why President Trump has seen record Republican turnout in the Iowa Caucuses and New Hampshire primary, and it's why we anticipate conservatives around the country to continue to demonstrate their support for this Administration all the way through Election Day in November. Help us keep up the momentum by making a contribution to help us defend the Republican Senate Majority that has confirmed over 180 of President Trump 's judicial nominees, delivered historic tax cuts for all Americans and worked tirelessly to advance President Trump's agenda! Donate using this secure link: The Democrats will continue to drag out their infighting and indecisiveness on just how far to the left they're willing to drag the party in order to regain power. Meanwhile, President Trump and the Republicans will continue to roll up their sleeves and get to work for the American people, and continue to deliver on promises made to boost our economy, get the country back to work and Make America Great Again! Thank you in advance for your commitment to the conservative cause, Peter. Your ongoing support is invaluable. Let's keep up the fight, Marsha Blackburn U.S. Senator (R-TN) **** Paid for by Blackburn Tennessee Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Marsha for Senate, MARSHA PAC, and Tennessee Republican Party Federal Election Account. Blackburn Tennessee Victory Fund PO Box 3750 Brentwood, TN 37024 This message reflects the opinions and representations of Blackburn Tennessee Victory Fund. You are receiving this email because you signed up as a member of Blackburn Tennessee Victory Fund's online community. ****

body:
| |
---

| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/15211816/160361256/664205141?refcode=em022020-header&refcode2=8f6b177a-ed53-ea11-a94c-00155d039e74&noskip=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NDI2NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZjZiMTc3YS1lZDUzLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=BJKdz4WlPJHCC1PMgpdMSBh1U9X0t4dhwocyKkQnNps=) **** | | Friend,

I’ve been touched by reading all of the birthday wishes you have sent to me. I want to thank you from the bottom of my heart.

When Trip and I seriously began discussing a Senate run last spring, we knew that running against an opponent with the president’s personal backing would be tough.

But it was tough running for office for the very first time, in a Central Georgia city during the Tea Party wave of 2010. We won that race, and it’s helped to prepare me for what was to come as I’ve traveled our state for the last ten months.

I’ve had the pleasure of meeting so many of you on this campaign, and I so deeply appreciate the work you are doing to make this campaign a success. I’m looking forward to meeting more of you, and representing you in Washington.

We are going to win this for you, and for all of us who are pouring our hearts and souls into righting the wrongs that have been done by this administration. For our immigrant neighbors, our children, our seniors, and working families. For our planet. All of us. We’re in it together.

Thank you again. I’m more encouraged now than I have ever been before. Let’s go.

Yours truly,
Teresa [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/15211817/160361257/664205141?refcode=em022020&noskip=true&refcode2=8f6b177a-ed53-ea11-a94c-00155d039e74&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NDI2NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZjZiMTc3YS1lZDUzLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=BJKdz4WlPJHCC1PMgpdMSBh1U9X0t4dhwocyKkQnNps=) | ************ ************

Dear Friend, The Young Democrats of Bryan/College Station (YNG/DMS/BCS) have issued endorsements for the first time and I'm proud to announce that I've earned their support! This one is particularly gratifying as the Young Dems BCS are an __extremely__ active group, responsible for registering many thousands of voters over the last two election cycles. This rounds out our portfolio of endorsements to include the key media organizations, labor organizations, traditional Democratic clubs, and younger Democratic clubs. Endorsements include:
* The Austin American-Statesman
* The Austin Chronicle
* Texas AFL-CIO (joint)
* Centex (Waco) Labor Council of the AFL-CIO
* Pflugerville Area Democrats
* North by Northwest Democrats
* Young Democrats BCS
* University Democrats (U. Texas)

Our endorsement strategy was simple and based on our 2018 experience: focus on the entities that have the most impact on voters in the district, and that fit into the general election strategy. We won every endorsement we competed for (save one which declined to endorse any candidate), and a couple we didn't. Day three of early voting is underway and signs are good. name: Nina Ahmad
subject: Press Release
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Nina for PA
body:
**Nina outpaces all Democratic candidates for PA Auditor General in petition drive, building momentum to defeat Trump and become first-state-wide elected executive of color in Commonwealth history**

Dr. Nina Ahmad, a path-breaking candidate for Pennsylvania's Auditor General seat, led all Democratic rivals, surpassing her nearest competitor by approximately 2,000 supporters in the open seat's state-wide petition drive.

With nearly 10,000 signatures (9726) collected, Nina energized dozens of diverse supporters from cities, towns, and regions throughout the state. The successful start-up to the campaign, officially puts Nina on the ballot in all sixty-seven counties for the Commonwealth's highly contested 2020 competitive primary that takes places on Tuesday, April 28th.

"I am so grateful to the dozens of volunteers--our dynamic and dedicated organizers from unions, small business, and the progressive and Democratic community. You each took personal time in all kinds of conditions to talk with neighbors, friends, and family about our mission. Thank you.

Our efforts will check Donald J. Trump.
When Trump is defeated, it will be thru this kind of robust organizing. As the leader in the Auditor General's office, I will serve as a catalyst and example, to empower communities and foster a more just future for Pennsylvania."

If elected, Nina would represent the **first woman of color** to serve at a state-wide-executive level in Pennsylvania's 233-year-old history. Dr. Ahmad would also serve as the only current state-wide executive women and person of color, if elected to succeed the current Auditor General Democrat Eugene DePasquale.

Amidst violent political turmoil in her country of Bangladesh, Nina immigrated to the U.S. on her own at 21. Nina put herself thru the University of Pennsylvania, earning a Ph.D as a scientist. With an impactful career, Nina has worked as a molecular biologist, advocate for women, and a small-business entrepreneur.

Nina has served as President to her local chapter of the National Organization for Woman (NOW) well as a Deputy Mayor to the Kenney Administration in Philadelphia where she launched the City's Commission for Women, managed the Office of Black Male Engagement, and the Youth Commission and worked closely with the office of LGBT Affairs.

Nina has also previously served at a national level on President Barack Obama's Advisory Commission on Asian Americans and Pacific Islanders.

Stated Senator Art Haywood of Montgomery County:

"Nina is clearly well-positioned to win this competitive election. Her intelligence, determination and record delivering change, makes her not only the strongest candidate for Auditor General but a critical voice to drive results for all Democrats and communities in Pennsylvania in 2020."

Learn more about Nina and this campaign [https://youtu.be/fmQmYg0BGOE](https://act.myngp.com/el/2HsAQAwuxxDxnc_xgD1lTjrbKS2LrXu9Fc2fcANQjjo=/UyZVF5TyCsEeUA-jBPBUzdHyeVOp9OVu6aS_ln5RfRM=?url=https%3A%2F%2Fyoutu.be%2FfmQmYg0BGOE&data=02%7C01%7C%7Ca345f30ce17a4bdf189208d7b541a96c%7C84df9e7fe9f640afb435aaaaaaaaaaaa%7C1%7C0%7C637177165919507910&sdata=QYPDIHbaxVcyZ2dgYfCpMkRwtl7uaJ8Dsumfmq8mfV0%3D&reserved=0)

To **give, volunteer or hear more** about Nina for pa visit us@ [www.ninaforpa.com](https://act.myngp.com/el/2HsAQAwuxxDxnc_xgD1lTjrbKS2LrXu9Fc2fcANQjjo=/nlC8g3WWvrVZQs-Y1ZDZA6FBlQzV5c_nzyPAYVGZiU4=).
Contact us at: [info@ninaforpa.com](https://act.myngp.com/el/2HsAQAwuxxDxnc_xgD1lTjrbKS2LrXu9Fc2fcANQjjo=/Uw-wwATVwOqc9RJHQt4jl1-yPuifz4omEvvR2JXur9o=); [facebook.com/ninaforpa](https://act.myngp.com/el/2HsAQAwuxxDxnc_xgD1lTjrbKS2LrXu9Fc2fcANQjjo=/7xcxFlG0m09CGlBl4SyjPK017BWsVSOcYWyO_J3xtjs=) or [twitter.com/ninaahmadphl](https://act.myngp.com/el/2HsAQAwuxxDxnc_xgD1lTjrbKS2LrXu9Fc2fcANQjjo=/m2xKm9OYC3gyjY1XvgnbqBQ91uGQS86qH-xYJbln-u4=)

name: Clarke Tucker
subject: Hey - have you voted?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Clarke Tucker for State Senate
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/EJ-ij4MN_sUmLbcR1iejUOnPMBSLdgTTg7f1ErW1sk8=/gNdXcQCdtV5El8_i0dx8ha0j4t7nz-QRdl5-jA1BAzU=)

Friend -

Early voting is underway in Arkansas, and I could not be more excited.

Seeing the lines outside the voting site doors, watching campaigners out supporting the candidates they're passionate about, witnessing our community engage in the very essence of democracy -- it brings joy to my heart and makes me proud to be an American.

Now, I tend to vote on Election Day with my family, but whatever your voting preference and whichever candidates you plan to cast your ballot for, I want you to promise me you will **go vote on or before the March 3rd Primary. **

You can find your [Pulaski County polling place here](https://act.myngp.com/el/EJ-ij4MN_sUmLbcR1iejUOnPMBSLdgTTg7f1ErW1sk8=/KVRtgGHp2DzpjDwzJKNH7GhBV4xSc8urkWr4mUbWLu8=).

And of course, I'm a candidate on the ballot for the Arkansas Legislature, and I could use your support to continue running a strong race. [**Please donate what you can to our campaign**](https://act.myngp.com/el/EJ-ij4MN_sUmLbcR1iejUOnPMBSLdgTTg7f1ErW1sk8=/gNdXcQCdtV5El8_i0dx8ha0j4t7nz-QRdl5-jA1BAzU=)**,** and together we will win this race on behalf of the hardworking families across our great state.

**I want Arkansas to have record turnout this year**. And that won't be possible if you don't get to the polls and bring your family, friends, and neighbors with you.

So, get out there and vote!** **[ And maybe donate.](https://act.myngp.com/el/EJ-ij4MN_sUmLbcR1iejUOnPMBSLdgTTg7f1ErW1sk8=/gNdXcQCdtV5El8_i0dx8ha0j4t7nz-QRdl5-jA1BAzU=) [**_But definitely vote._**](https://act.myngp.com/el/EJ-ij4MN_sUmLbcR1iejUOnPMBSLdgTTg7f1ErW1sk8=/KVRtgGHp2DzpjDwzJKNH7GhBV4xSc8urkWr4mUbWLu8=)

Thanks,

Clarke

I guess we shouldn’t be surprised given his disappointing record: ➡️ Voting AGAINST the Violence Against Women Act
➡️ Calling on the Supreme Court to reconsider Roe v Wade **Bottom line: Scott Perry is bad for Pennsylvania’s women.**

[**The good news is it doesn’t have to be this way, friend. As Auditor General, Eugene has stood up time and time again for women and families. He has nearly eliminated the backlog of rape kits and made a commitment to tackling that issue nationwide in Congress. He also understands that a woman should be able to make decisions about her own body and has earned the endorsement of NARAL Pro-Choice America for exactly that reason.

**I’m proud to be on Team Eugene, and I know that he is the type of representative who will go to Congress and continue to fight for the issues impacting all of us.**

body:

Peter,

On Tuesday night, I appeared on NY1 with Errol Louis to discuss my run for Congress. Specifically, I laid out my proposal to construct 12 million affordable housing units nationwide and talked about why now, more than ever, we need leaders in office who'll prioritize solving our affordable housing crisis from day one. **[Will you pitch in $200, $100, or $50 today so we can put our plan into action?](https://act.myngp.com/el/MbOY4WeKFh2L_V495NrmCmgjvI-SFfZF8WjU2e55x-c=/Odlu-aSFQaHFebqUdFPw6xYKR-yG6PORk3S-Gc1C3_Q=)**
