******[Express Donate: $100](https://act.myngp.com/el/MbOY4WeKFh2L_V495NrmCmgjvI-SFfZF8WjU2e55x-c=/Odlu-aSFQaHFebqUdFPw6xYKR-yG6PORk3S-Gc1C3_Q=?express_lane=true&amount=100&refcode=express_lane_100)******
******[Express Donate: $50](https://act.myngp.com/el/MbOY4WeKFh2L_V495NrmCmgjvI-SFfZF8WjU2e55x-c=/Odlu-aSFQaHFebqUdFPw6xYKR-yG6PORk3S-Gc1C3_Q=?express_lane=true&amount=50&refcode=express_lane_50)******
******[Or, donate another amount](https://act.myngp.com/el/MbOY4WeKFh2L_V495NrmCmgjvI-SFfZF8WjU2e55x-c=/Odlu-aSFQaHFebqUdFPw6xYKR-yG6PORk3S-Gc1C3_Q=?express_lane_other=true&refcode=express_lane_other)******

Just days ago, the investigative news site City Limits published an article that shows how Rep. Clarke's record on housing stacks up against my policy positions -- and let's just say, it's pretty one-sided. The author points out that, "Of the 235 press releases [Rep. Clarke's] office has issued over the past five years, only five were connected to housing," while my vision is "more ambitious than even the most aggressive of the presidential candidates."

We're calling for a homes guarantee, a tenants' Bill of Rights, greater investments in public housing, a "right to counsel" for renters facing eviction, and more. But over the last 13 years that Rep. Clarke has served in office, she hasn't called for much of anything. I'm taking an aggressive approach because I believe that the millions of families who are struggling to maintain permanent housing deserve nothing less. **[If you agree, will you support my fight for safe, accessible, and affordable housing today?](https://act.myngp.com/el/MbOY4WeKFh2L_V495NrmCmgjvI-SFfZF8WjU2e55x-c=/Odlu-aSFQaHFebqUdFPw6xYKR-yG6PORk3S-Gc1C3_Q=)**

Best,
Adem

******[DONATE TODAY](https://act.myngp.com/el/MbOY4WeKFh2L_V495NrmCmgjvI-SFfZF8WjU2e55x-c=/Odlu-aSFQaHFebqUdFPw6xYKR-yG6PORk3S-Gc1C3_Q=)******

P.S. You can find the full text of the article published in City Limits below.

In Election Rematch, Brooklyn Congressional Candidates Serve Up Housing Policy

The spotlight on housing issues is brighter in 2020 than in any election year in recent memory. Just about all of the top Democratic presidential candidates are pitching national housing plans. Mayor Bill de Blasio, who six years ago launched the largest housing-development initiative in the country, now says the city's deepest housing needs still need addressing. With housing stress evident everywhere from the streets of L.A. to mobile home parks in Nebraska, housing might be the pocketbook issue that connects "red state" and "blue state" voters.

It could also be the defining issue in the race to represent New York's Ninth Congressional district, which covers a chunk of Brooklyn running from Prospect Park and Crown Heights down to Sheepshead Bay.

Two years ago, rookie candidate Adem Bunkeddeko challenged Congresswoman Yvette Clarke and came within 1,852 votes of winning--equivalent to one half of one percent of voters in the district. The two will meet again in this June's Democratic primary, in which the other declared candidates are Councilmember Chaim Deutsch, small business owner Lutchi Gayot, technology entrepreneur Alexander Hubbard and community organizer and Iraq/Afghanistan veteran Isiah James.

Bunkeddeko opened his second attempt to unseat Clarke with a length policy paper on housing that framed what he calls an "American Homes Guarantee" that would treat housing as a basic right.

In a more modest move, Clarke last year proposed a legislative fix to the way target incomes for "affordable housing" developments are set--a long-standing pet peeve of housing advocates in the city.

One thing that is likely is that housing affordability is a challenge facing many of those voters. About 70 percent of households in the district are renters. According to the American Community Survey, 53 percent of those renter households in the district pay more than 30 percent of their income in rent, up from 49 percent of renters in 2005.

The challenger's plan

Bunkeddeko--who until recently worked for the Local Initiatives Support Corporation, a nonprofit that helps facilitate financing for housing and other development in low-income neighborhoods--says his "homes guarantee" would "ensure that every family can live in safe, accessible, and permanently affordable housing."

His plan revolves around the construction of 12 million units of below-market housing through community land trusts operated by nonprofits or resident cooperatives.

Bunkeddeko also wants to build more senior and supportive housing. He says Washington should fork over the "$32 billion that the federal government owes NYCHA," and he wants to clear legal barriers to the building of new public housing. Under the Homes Guarantee plan, the U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development would be empowered to investigate speculators and levy taxes on vacant property. He also backs an existing scheme that would create national rent control and a "right to counsel" for tenants facing eviction.

To boost homeownership in communities that "have been historically blocked from buying homes," Bunkeddeko's plan also calls for deeper down-payment support and the creation of a Community Opportunity to Purchase (COPA) program.

Put simply, the plan calls for creating a lot of housing, starting with the 12 million-unit target. By way of comparison, New York State and Ohio together host about 12 million housing units, the Low Income Housing Tax Credit has financed about 2 million homes since that mechanism was created in 1986 and there are 1.2 million public housing apartments in the country. Most estimates of the country's housing shortage put it at about 7 million units, although that need will grow as population grows (the U.S. has 40 million more people now than it did in 2000).

"Given Washington's devastating disinvestment in public housing, it is going to take a real commitment, well over $100 Billion a year for the next ten years or more to try to right this ship," Bunkeddeko tells City Limits. "But we are past the point of this being optional; families in NYCHA are living in third-world conditions and homelessness is at its highest level since the Great Depression. It's time for Congress to act."

Bunkeddeko's vision is more ambitious than even the most aggressive of the presidential candidates: Sen. Bernie Sanders has proposed building 10 million units and former Mayor Pete Buttigieg 9 million.

On one hand, that makes his plan unique. On the other, it means passing it would be a heavy left for a freshman member of Congress.

"It's exciting that folks running for president, particularly Democrats, are talking about housing," Bunkeddeko says. He believes his 2018 race, where housing was a focus, helped shape the conversation that the presidential contenders are now amplifying. "I believe there is a great deal of energy and work to put in if we are elected, because there is a lot of work to do."

The record and the proposal

The race two years ago was easily the toughest Clarke had faced since 2006, when she won a four-way Democratic primary to succeed Major Owens, who'd held the seat for 24 years. The legendary Shirley Chisholm was Owens' predecessor in the district.

In Clarke's first term, spanning 2007 and 2008, she introduced the Real Estate Assessment Center Inspection Improvement Act and the Foreclosure Prevention Act of 2008. The following session, for 2009-2010, she reintroduced the Foreclosure Prevention Act and wrote the Affordable Housing and Community Development Act.

None of those proposals appear to have made it beyond committee, but they reflected some attempt to deal with housing worries. During the same time period, Clarke was somewhat visible as a voice on NYCHA, telling the Daily News in 2010: "I'm in Washington fighting . . . to get it right and make sure that New Yorkers of modest and low income can afford to stay in this place."

More recently, however, Clarke's attention--at least as a law-writer--moved elsewhere; her proposals tended to focus on terrorism, small businesses, immigration and other issues, although there was a 2014 bill to assist homeowners hit by disasters. Of the 235 press releases her office has issued over the past five years, only five were connected to housing.

It wasn't until 2018 that she proposed another broad housing bill: the Hardest Hit Housing Act, which "would provide additional funds for various housing programs, including grants to large public housing agencies for specified capital activities, grants for foreclosure mitigation counseling and legal assistance, and incremental vouchers for rental assistance for large public housing agencies," a Clarke spokeswoman says.

When she returned to Washington after the tough 2018 race, Clarke re-introduced that measure and two other proposed laws, the No Biometric Barriers to Housing Act and the Affordable Housing and Area Median Income Fairness Act.

Aiming at AMI

That last proposal attracted some attention last fall, because Area Median Income (AMI) has been a feature of almost every debate about affordable housing, rezoning and development in the city.

The federal tax credit and subsidy programs that underwrite most affordable housing peg eligibility to AMI. In New York City's case, an odd legislative history means AMI is calculated on a region-wide basis, with Rockland and Westchester counties thrown in. Those skew the underlying numbers. Just as important, the feds adjust AMIs upward to account for high housing costs in the city. Taken together, it often means that "affordable housing" is priced out of reach of the families who need it most.

Clarke's bill (which has 22 co-sponsors) would address both problems--and, importantly, appears to target the housing-cost adjustments only to how tax credits are used. That means her bill avoids lowering eligibility thresholds for Section 8 and public housing, which would exclude thousands of New Yorkers. The proposed law would also increase funding for affordable housing by $2.5 billion every year for a decade. A spokeswoman for Clarke says the bill "aims to cut rents in subsidized developments in the five boroughs by more than a third."

While those aspects of the bill address key concerns, its overall impact could be complicated, since affordable housing is typically financed using multiple funding streams. Also, lower income levels serve needier people, but they also generate lower rent revenue to support housing developments over time. "Many programs are built around federal standards and fit together like a puzzle piece," one housing expect told City Limits. "To truly understand the impact, there would need to be a thorough analysis of which programs would be impacted and how it might impact the cost of development."

Bunkaddeko dismisses Clarke's AMI bill. "It's too little, too late and doesn't address the issues at hand," he says. "AMI is just a number or an index. The structural issue is that there isn't enough affordable housing available here."

Clarke's office did not make the congresswoman available for an interview. In a statement, a Clarke spokeswoman said: "While Brooklyn's economic make-up has shifted dramatically during Congresswoman's Clarke's time in office, she has been a relentless voice to ensure that Brooklyn remains affordable for everyday working families, including low-income communities." New housing proposals are coming, the spokeswoman says.

Along with many in the New York delegation, Clarke is a co-sponsor of Velzaquez's Public Housing Emergency Response Act, which would authorize $70 billion to shore up public housing around the country.

A race takes shape

On the campaign trail, Clarke maintains a lead in fundraising--she has $344,000 on hand compared with Bunkeddeko's $189,000--but it is not clear if she will be able to achieve the financial advantage she had in 2018. Over the course of that cycle, Clarke spent $879,000 to Bunkeddeko's $259,000, although not all of the congresswoman's spending was concentrated on the primary campaign. (Clarke faced only token Republican opposition in the 2018 general election, winning with 87 percent of the vote.)

Deutsch's campaign did not respond to multiple emails requesting comment on his own housing-policy plans. Gayot's agenda highlights the potential of modular housing to help close the gap between supply and demand, and stresses the importance of transit infrastructure to making distant neighborhoods viable as housing hubs. Hubbard's campaign website features the issue but offers few specifics.

James offers the most detailed plan to rival Bunkeddeko's. It calls for taxes on non-primary homes, support for land trusts, federal backing for low- and middle-income housing cooperatives, and a crackdown on AirBnB, among other ideas.

Copyright © 2020 Friends of Adem, All rights reserved. You are receiving this email because you have subscribed to emails from Adem for Congress, made a donation to or volunteered for the campaign, or are a friend of Adem Bunkeddeko.
---
Friends of Adem
P.O. Box 130-427
Brooklyn NY 11213-0427 United States
If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please [unsubscribe](https://act.myngp.com/el/MbOY4WeKFh2L_V495NrmCmgjvI-SFfZF8WjU2e55x-c=/qu4WCs5Owru2m9SMsTZ9tkTT6ul_ebfGM4cJac49kz8=).

The record and the proposal The race two years ago was easily the toughest Clarke had faced since 2006, when she won a four-way Democratic primary to succeed Major Owens, who'd held the seat for 24 years. The legendary Shirley Chisholm was Owens' predecessor in the district. In Clarke's first term, spanning 2007 and 2008, she introduced the Real Estate Assessment Center Inspection Improvement Act and the Foreclosure Prevention Act of 2008. The following session, for 2009-2010, she reintroduced the Foreclosure Prevention Act and wrote the Affordable Housing and Community Development Act. None of those proposals appear to have made it beyond committee, but they reflected some attempt to deal with housing worries. During the same time period, Clarke was somewhat visible as a voice on NYCHA, telling the Daily News in 2010: "I'm in Washington fighting . . . to get it right and make sure that New Yorkers of modest and low income can afford to stay in this place." More recently, however, Clarke's attention--at least as a law-writer--moved elsewhere; her proposals tended to focus on terrorism, small businesses, immigration and other issues, although there was a 2014 bill to assist homeowners hit by disasters. Of the 235 press releases her office has issued over the past five years, only five were connected to housing. It wasn't until 2018 that she proposed another broad housing bill: the Hardest Hit Housing Act, which "would provide additional funds for various housing programs, including grants to large public housing agencies for specified capital activities, grants for foreclosure mitigation counseling and legal assistance, and incremental vouchers for rental assistance for large public housing agencies," a Clarke spokeswoman says. When she returned to Washington after the tough 2018 race, Clarke re-introduced that measure and two other proposed laws, the No Biometric Barriers to Housing Act and the Affordable Housing and Area Median Income Fairness Act. Aiming at AMI That last proposal attracted some attention last fall, because Area Median Income (AMI) has been a feature of almost every debate about affordable housing, rezoning and development in the city. The federal tax credit and subsidy programs that underwrite most affordable housing peg eligibility to AMI. In New York City's case, an odd legislative history means AMI is calculated on a region-wide basis, with Rockland and Westchester counties thrown in. Those skew the underlying numbers. Just as important, the feds adjust AMIs upward to account for high housing costs in the city. Taken together, it often means that "affordable housing" is priced out of reach of the families who need it most. Clarke's bill (which has 22 co-sponsors) would address both problems--and, importantly, appears to target the housing-cost adjustments only to how tax credits are used. That means her bill avoids lowering eligibility thresholds for Section 8 and public housing, which would exclude thousands of New Yorkers. The proposed law would also increase funding for affordable housing by $2.5 billion every year for a decade. A spokeswoman for Clarke says the bill "aims to cut rents in subsidized developments in the five boroughs by more than a third." While those aspects of the bill address key concerns, its overall impact could be complicated, since affordable housing is typically financed using multiple funding streams. Also, lower income levels serve needier people, but they also generate lower rent revenue to support housing developments over time. "Many programs are built around federal standards and fit together like a puzzle piece," one housing expect told City Limits. "To truly understand the impact, there would need to be a thorough analysis of which programs would be impacted and how it might impact the cost of development." Bunkaddeko dismisses Clarke's AMI bill. "It's too little, too late and doesn't address the issues at hand," he says. "AMI is just a number or an index. The structural issue is that there isn't enough affordable housing available here." Clarke's office did not make the congresswoman available for an interview. In a statement, a Clarke spokeswoman said: "While Brooklyn's economic make-up has shifted dramatically during Congresswoman's Clarke's time in office, she has been a relentless voice to ensure that Brooklyn remains affordable for everyday working families, including low-income communities." New housing proposals are coming, the spokeswoman says. Along with many in the New York delegation, Clarke is a co-sponsor of Velzaquez's Public Housing Emergency Response Act, which would authorize $70 billion to shore up public housing around the country. A race takes shape On the campaign trail, Clarke maintains a lead in fundraising--she has $344,000 on hand compared with Bunkeddeko's $189,000--but it is not clear if she will be able to achieve the financial advantage she had in 2018. Over the course of that cycle, Clarke spent $879,000 to Bunkeddeko's $259,000, although not all of the congresswoman's spending was concentrated on the primary campaign. (Clarke faced only token Republican opposition in the 2018 general election, winning with 87 percent of the vote.) Deutsch's campaign did not respond to multiple emails requesting comment on his own housing-policy plans. Gayot's agenda highlights the potential of modular housing to help close the gap between supply and demand, and stresses the importance of transit infrastructure to making distant neighborhoods viable as housing hubs. Hubbard's campaign website features the issue but offers few specifics. James offers the most detailed plan to rival Bunkeddeko's. It calls for taxes on non-primary homes, support for land trusts, federal backing for low- and middle-income housing cooperatives, and a crackdown on AirBnB, among other ideas. Copyright © 2020 Friends of Adem, All rights reserved. Box 130-427 Brooklyn NY 11213-0427 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please =.

name: =?utf-8?Q?Juli=C3=A1n_Castro?=
subject: Why I'm endorsing Marie Newman
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Julian for the Future
body:
This won't be an easy fight. But it's one we can - and must - win. | | |
---
| |
---
| | Friend, After I ended my campaign for president, I made a promise that I wouldn't stop fighting for bold and progressive policies and candidates to move our country forward. **[That's why today, I'm so thrilled to endorse Marie Newman for Congress in Illinois' Third Congressional District.](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fc92f/3eb3e2fc/6a3c0997/744eb59c/4190978648/VEsH/?g=5-wKdTjfd5lcsCTt4S4B-7A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/q1email?refcode=julian-marie&utm_medium=email&utm_source=juliancastro&utm_content=1+-+Thatrsquos+why+today+Irsquom+so+thrilled&utm_campaign=02192020EM&source=02192020EM")** Marie has been a lifelong fighter for hardworking families like the one she grew up in. Instead of playing Washington politics, I know that she'll work to ensure the people she represents can get better health care and a good education--and that immigrants will be protected from Trump's cruel and inhumane agenda. **This election is in just 26 days, and Marie is up against an eight-term incumbent, machine politics, and a whole host of D.C. special interests. She 's an underdog who needs our support -- ****[Will you chip in to support her campaign right now?](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fc92f/3eb3e2fc/6a3c0997/744eb59b/4190978648/VEsE/?g=5-wKdTjfd5lcsCTt4S4B-7A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/q1email?refcode=julian-marie&utm_medium=email&utm_source=juliancastro&utm_content=2+-+Will+you+chip+in+to+support+her+campaign&utm_campaign=02192020EM&source=02192020EM")** You might be wondering why I'm weighing in on a race like this one. It's because so much is at stake in this election. At a time when Donald Trump is hellbent on attacking immigrants, targeting Americans' health care, and undermining a woman's right to choose--we need champions in Washington who will stand up for our values and protect the most vulnerable among us. Marie Newman will do just that, and that's why I'm proud to support her. **Last cycle, Marie fell short by just 2%. We can't let that happen again.** **[Make a donation to her campaign right now>>](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fc92f/3eb3e2fc/6a3c0997/744eb593/4190978648/VEsF/?g=5-wKdTjfd5lcsCTt4S4B-7A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/q1email?refcode=julian-marie&utm_medium=email&utm_source=juliancastro&utm_content=3+-+Make+a+donation+to+her+campaign+right+no&utm_campaign=02192020EM&source=02192020EM")** Thanks, Julian [**Donate**](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fc92f/3eb3e2fc/6a3c0997/744eb593/4190978648/VEsC/?g=5-wKdTjfd5lcsCTt4S4B-7A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/q1email?refcode=julian-marie&utm_medium=email&utm_source=juliancastro&utm_content=3+-+Make+a+donation+to+her+campaign+right+no&utm_campaign=02192020EM&source=02192020EM")
---

subject: Donald Trump and Mitch McConnell fully support my opponent

body:
The Washington Post named our race in Michigan as one of the GOP's top targets.
| | |
---
Friends, I'll cut right to it: The Washington Post named our race in Michigan as one of the GOP's top targets. And recent polling shows me tied with my right-wing opponent in a race that could decide Senate control.
Our deadline is tonight, but right now, we're falling short of our $30,000 match goal. That's why a generous group of donors is matching all gifts - dollar for dollar, but only until midnight.

**Friends, I wouldn't ask if it weren't important:[Please, can you give $5 or more and have every dollar doubled to defend Michigan's Senate seat and take back the Senate for Democrats?](https://secure.petersformichigan.com/page/m/7b6da854/5721a3ed/70b409ca/efb16f4/1686263110/VEsH/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoicGV0ZXJzX2VtYWlsIiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJqdGsyODctZnItaW4tbmQiLCJlM3NnVEZCQlFWRjFaWEo1VTNSeWFXNW5JSDE5IjoiNSwyNSw1MCwxMDAsMjUwLDUwMCIsImUzc2dZV0p5WldOMWNuSnBibWNnZlgwPSI6IiZyZWN1cnJpbmc9MSJ9/?g=SkKTSlNw52hcsC2e00JiNrA "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/peters_email?refcode=jtk287-fr-in-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&recurring=1")**
My opponent has the full backing of Donald Trump and Mitch McConnell, who are already helping him raise _millions_ to defeat me. And just last week, we learned Betsy DeVos' family is dumping _NEARLY ONE MILLION_ dollars in an attempt to buy this seat.

The future of the Senate is on the line, so falling short just isn't an option. I'm counting on you to come through, friends. **Will you give $5 or more to help us win in Michigan and take back the Senate for Democrats?**
**[Give $5 or more right now- before tonight's midnight deadline - and have every dollar doubled to defend Michigan's Senate seat, take back the Senate and protect the future of the Supreme Court.](https://secure.petersformichigan.com/page/m/7b6da854/5721a3ed/70b409ca/efb16f4/1686263110/VEsE/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoicGV0ZXJzX2VtYWlsIiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJqdGsyODctZnItaW4tbmQiLCJlM3NnVEZCQlFWRjFaWEo1VTNSeWFXNW5JSDE5IjoiNSwyNSw1MCwxMDAsMjUwLDUwMCIsImUzc2dZV0p5WldOMWNuSnBibWNnZlgwPSI6IiZyZWN1cnJpbmc9MSJ9/?g=SkKTSlNw52hcsC2e00JiNrA "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/peters_email?refcode=jtk287-fr-in-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&recurring=1")**
Thank you,

Gary

name: MJ Hegar Campaign HQ
subject: our new TV ad!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by MJ for Texas
body:
We're facing a major wiring deadline and we need your help to fund our ad.

|
---
[](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/6489056f/7f51f7bc/4ddb66ef/4ef1befd/1782729552/VEsH/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/logo?refcode=emaillogo&amount=5")

| | | [](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/6489056f/7f51f7bc/4ddb66ef/4ef1befc/1782729552/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6Ijk0MiJ9/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-feb-wiring-deadline?refcode=email_20200220.1nd__942__&amount=10&recurring=true")
---
This is URGENT, Peter, so we'll get straight to the point: We just launched our new TV ad to reach undecided voters before the Texas primary, but we still need to raise $22,000 before tonight's wiring deadline!

**[Will you rush a donation right now to make sure we have the resources to fund this critical ad buy?](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/6489056f/7f51f7bc/4ddb66ef/4ef1befc/1782729552/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6Ijk0MiJ9/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-feb-wiring-deadline?refcode=email_20200220.1nd__942__&amount=10&recurring=true")**

Sen. John Cornyn aired the first TV ads of the race _months_ ago, and the Koch network ran ads supporting his reelection campaign too. **But unlike Sen. Cornyn, we don 't have an army of corporate PACs ready to write checks at a moment's notice. | Hey Peter,

Imagine just for a minute that we were on Ross Spano’s team. We wouldn’t really need your support since we’d have our wealthy, corporate PAC friends to donate to us. But since Spano has been in some hot water lately over illegal campaign contributions, we’d be grateful for the good PR. We’d also tell you about Spano’s big goals over the next term, such as promising to:
* Double the number of his scandals
* Continue to deny the existence of climate change
* Put the interests of our shady, corporate donors above that of our community
* Make FL-15 the laughing stock of the nation

_Okay, Peter,_ we’re sorry but we just couldn’t help ourselves. How is this man our representative? Common sense would tell us that anyone would be a better representative than Ross Spano, and fortunately we don’t have just anyone. subject: Deputy Sheriffs' Association Endorses John Hutchison
body:
| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/15228122/160505458/-1172787226?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTE3MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYjQwY2FkNy0yMTU0LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=fMYGktE1pJ6Ifmb8y_xHE9Ts4s951b1I9tizwdR5Rbo=&emci=1ab82b25-8053-ea11-a94c-00155d039e74&emdi=2b40cad7-2154-ea11-a94c-00155d039e74&ceid=4846285) |
---|---
| |
---
Earlier this week Justice Hutchison received the endorsement of the WV Deputy Sheriffs' Association. "It is an honor to have the endorsement of our state's Deputy Sheriffs' Association. These officers put their lives on the line each day and they deserve a judicial system that works as hard as they do for the people of the Mountain State." [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/15228123/160505459/-1172787226?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTE3MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYjQwY2FkNy0yMTU0LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=fMYGktE1pJ6Ifmb8y_xHE9Ts4s951b1I9tizwdR5Rbo=&emci=1ab82b25-8053-ea11-a94c-00155d039e74&emdi=2b40cad7-2154-ea11-a94c-00155d039e74&ceid=4846285) The Association's Secretary John O'Dell of Nicholas County said, "the WV Deputy Sheriff Association has voted unanimously to endorse you for the upcoming 2020 Primary election. We wish you good luck" Justice Hutchison is committed to restoring integrity to the West Virginia Supreme Court. He is working tirelessly to earn the trust of our citizens. Having the support of our men and women in uniform helps that effort. Will you support the effort? Will you donate today? If you have saved your payment information with FastAction, your contribution will go through immediately on clicking a link. clean_body: | | | | ---|--- | | Earlier this week Justice Hutchison received the endorsement of the WV Deputy Sheriffs' Association. "It is an honor to have the endorsement of our state's Deputy Sheriffs' Association. These officers put their lives on the line each day and they deserve a judicial system that works as hard as they do for the people of the Mountain State." The Association's Secretary John O'Dell of Nicholas County said, "the WV Deputy Sheriff Association has voted unanimously to endorse you for the upcoming 2020 Primary election. We wish you good luck" Justice Hutchison is committed to restoring integrity to the West Virginia Supreme Court. He is working tirelessly to earn the trust of our citizens. Having the support of our men and women in uniform helps that effort. Will you support the effort? Will you donate today? If you have saved your payment information with FastAction, your contribution will go through immediately on clicking a link. | | Connect With Us: | | Contact Info: Committee to Keep Justice Hutchison PO Box 5176 Beckley, WV 25801 United States | ---|--- If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please .

name: Progressive Majority
subject: Help us raise the minimum wage TODAY!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Progressive Majority PAC
body:
|
---

| | Hi Peter \-- 7 months ago, the House passed the Raise the Wage Act. It would be the first increase in the national minimum wage since 2009(!), but the Senate is currently refusing to act. 56 million workers in the 21 states that haven't raised the minimum wage on their own make only $7.25 an hour. And, thanks to inflation, that $7.25 is worth 17% _LESS_ than it was in 2009. According to the Congressional Budget Office, raising the wage would lift 1.3 million Americans out of poverty, including 600,000 children. We can't let this opportunity go by. [**If you believe the Senate needs to act, please join us and other progressive Democrats across the country in demanding the Senate pass this bill. We need 50,000 signatures -- will you be part of the movement?**](https://act.progressivemajoritypac.com/page/m/3f9a5c54/5360b1c8/2d483894/b0711ad/2263472791/VEsH/?g=RdxxUK0LBwxeO7oKxQx0RTA "https://petitions.signforgood.com/raise-the-min-wage/?code=ProgMaj&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fs.surveyplanet.com%2FIdMp_Txx") Thank you for everything you do for the cause, Progressive Majority | **[SIGN THE PETITION](https://act.progressivemajoritypac.com/page/m/3f9a5c54/5360b1c8/2d483894/b0711ad/2263472791/VEsE/?g=RdxxUK0LBwxeO7oKxQx0RTA "https://petitions.signforgood.com/raise-the-min-wage/?code=ProgMaj&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fs.surveyplanet.com%2FIdMp_Txx")**

The response to my message yesterday was so incredible and I wanted to share it with you again below. I think it helps explain how important this moment really is for our grassroots campaign. Thanks for all you do. -- John

* * *

Hello --
I'm writing to ask you to make a contribution to my Senate campaign today. This is an important moment for our team -- and we need your help. Here's the situation:
Donald Trump is coming to Colorado tomorrow. He's hosting a rally and a fundraiser for our opponent as a public thank you for blocking witnesses and voting to acquit him during the impeachment trial.
Here's the truth: Trump will very likely attack me personally on stage tomorrow evening. Even if he doesn't, his right-wing allies will raise huge sums of money for Cory Gardner, and they'll spend it on vicious attack ads attempting to tear me down personally with lies and insults.
That's why we're in the middle of an emergency fundraising drive to raise more than $250,000 online from grassroots donors before Trump takes the stage tomorrow. Each dollar helps make sure we're ready to fight back against whatever Trump and the GOP hurl at us this week.
**Make a $10 donation to our campaign before Trump 's Colorado rally tomorrow. Our race is said to be the best shot Democrats have at flipping a GOP Senate seat, so your donation really matters. Chip in now:**
_If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_
| [CHIP IN $10 NOW](https://secure.hickenlooper.%3D/?g=Wvb-QcQot1BcsCb456pUfvA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jwh-em-200219?refcode=em-200219-bump-full&amount=10&express_lane=true&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&employer=&occupation=")

John

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: President Trump’s Approval Rating Among Small Business Owners Hits All-Time High
body:
"Sixty-four percent of small business owners approve of the way Donald Trump is handling his job as president, the highest approval rating..." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e5986d4a06&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **President Trump’s Approval Rating Among Small Business Owners Hits All-Time High**

“Sixty-four percent of small business owners approve of the way Donald Trump is handling his job as president, the highest approval rating for Trump among entrepreneurs since CNBC and SurveyMonkey began conducting a quarterly survey in 2017,” Riley de Leon reports for CNBC.

“This is a high watermark for President Trump’s job approval, both among small business owners in our survey and among the general public,” said SurveyMonkey senior research scientist Laura Wronski.

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=2c364be58e&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| President Trump kept a key promise to California’s agriculture community yesterday. “For too long water authorities have flushed millions of gallons into the Pacific,” the President said to a crowd of 3,000 farmers and residents in Bakersfield. “I ordered the administration to update outdated opinions which determined water allocation in this state,” Alex Tavlian reports in [_The San Joaquin Valley Sun_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=337b188876&e=3ee4985eea).
---
|
---
| “Hosted by Her Highness Sheikha Manal bint Mohammed al Maktoum and the Dubai Women's Establishment, [this week’s] Global Women's Forum in Dubai saw Ivanka Trump take to the stage as a keynote speaker, celebrating just over a year since she launched the Global Women's Development and Prosperity Initiative,” _**[Harper’s Bazaar Arabia](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=21a4236638&e=3ee4985eea) **_reports.
---
|
---
| Former prisoners will have a chance to meet President Trump at a graduation ceremony today in Las Vegas. The program, “Hope for Prisoners,” helps former inmates re-enter the workforce. “CEO Jon Ponder says this presidential visit will really help those former prisoners realize that the American dream is still possible,” Austin Carter reports for [KTNV](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=511de0e856&e=3ee4985eea).

[WATCH President Trump live at 2:45 p.m. ET.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4be50f85fe&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=33114c80f8&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8a013a65b5&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=ab77e0018b)

name: Talia Marcus
subject: My first job
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mondaire for Congress_ Our mailing address is:
body:
I grew up in White Plains, right in the heart of New York’s 17th Congressional District. So I can’t tell you how amazing of an experience it has been for my first job out of college to be working to elect Mondaire as my next member of Congress. | | | [](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/mps2/c/5wA/ni0YAA/t.2zf/jG-sIYUDQTKsj_CakxU0qg/h0/hhRpZZW1RfrqdNP9De00vQRlUIk-2BKhU3wA9r6CKglNJhJQzIgEqsDHH-2FMFGK5qWR-2BdlqmZWfXH7KNdlnyzc4EAVQO6ebr8ucfO0L7f1wAMo3DEqyJnliP87bfdbBc0ahLMSmROeMBxgJr6Bn3IID4VUFDur-2BpeSqHK5nJCtEkQxouSkUkZlsgCw-2Fn8bJOAXV-2BRP8BbeI4AquRPlP0NsBlAqNJto4vsCGqduV35VL6HZSIScW6ps1YKtpXVqlstM4/8Hno)
Friend, **** **** **** I grew up in White Plains, right in the heart of New York’s 17th Congressional District. So I can’t tell you how amazing of an experience it has been for my first job out of college to be working to elect Mondaire as my next member of Congress.
I joined the team back in October, and since then have been helping out with press, social media, fundraising, and whatever else needs to get done around the office. It’s been an incredible opportunity to learn how a campaign works and to travel all across my hometown district . . . even if it means moving back in with my parents. This experience means so much to me because I truly believe in Mondaire’s vision of enacting the big, bold solutions that we need to ensure a better world for everyone.
One thing I’ve learned so far is just how expensive campaigns are, and how crucial fundraising is. Working for a candidate that doesn’t accept corporate PAC money or self-fund his campaign, I know that emails like this are what help us raise the money we need to be competitive in this race. **_[Can you chip in with a contribution today to help support me in getting Mondaire elected as my next member of Congress?](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/mps2/c/5wA/ni0YAA/t.2zf/jG-sIYUDQTKsj_CakxU0qg/h1/hhRpZZW1RfrqdNP9De00vQRlUIk-2BKhU3wA9r6CKglNJhJQzIgEqsDHH-2FMFGK5qWR-2BdlqmZWfXH7KNdlnyzc4EDZXp9eDoSPW8JGC7oV-2B3jOtL-2BrTR3srgBZHECp8NisgSRWMLrGQyLKNwEvRxwVTzQWEg8v7SCgVW3-2B8l0J-2Fn7nZbf3Dw0-2FOaL1EbiUsem-2Bf4dSIqtezvRn4cmNHDec151Tf6pANubhcNHfZfxFMNaJRSZu3q8q2mJfXuEaE9JDE/ks-7)_** **_[](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/mps2/c/5wA/ni0YAA/t.2zf/jG-sIYUDQTKsj_CakxU0qg/h2/hhRpZZW1RfrqdNP9De00vQRlUIk-2BKhU3wA9r6CKglNI1l0o3rFdF8DguS5dA2UxJBDNl2lgCvklUerujd6OQv2UAJ81FO6JHi2rGATfvNCC3PPdpAIfXT4zDWdpHpUsAcQOp5OzXyrRc9mWzjEHoJikvYB0-2BKme0qgwnvzBKJbA-2FmF7GTKihvcQ9tjSk37NTwUf7AikO9OoiNVwVr2p-2BM-2FOVSp9ziuOBvGOVB6mBbWE-3D/gXjR)_**

name: Senator Jeff Merkley
subject: Adorable animal photos and videos
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jeff Merkley for Oregon
body:
| [](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2f155/6cd27893/53f83a84/1586851d/1654793680/VEsH/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://www.jeffmerkley.com")
---

| | There's something magical about seeing children discover the wonders of the animal world. And there's a reason cat videos and other animal photos and videos are our favorite internet content. We have a special relationship with animals, but that relationship is going horribly awry. We're in the middle of an extinction crisis, and **too many animals are endangered, at risk of disappearing from our Earth entirely.** [****](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2f155/6cd27893/53f83a84/1586851a/1654793680/VEsE/p/eyJlM3RoYzJ0OWZRPT0iOiIzIiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjl2Ym1WOWZRPT0iOiI1IiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjkwZDI5OWZRPT0iOiIxNSIsImUzdGhZbUZ0ZEY5MGFISmxaWDE5IjoiMjUiLCJlM3RoWW1GdGRGOW1iM1Z5ZlgwPSI6IjUwIiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjltYVhabGZYMD0iOiIxMDAifQ%3D%3D/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "http://secure.actblue.com/donate/stand-with-jeff-merkley?refcode=2020-02-19-wildlife-e&recurring=1&amount=3&amounts=5,15,25,50,100") Unlawful trafficking and poaching. Climate chaos. Eroding habitats. So many things have gone wrong. _The African elephant:_ in a disastrous poaching stretch between 2006 and 2015, more than 100,000 died. _The White Rhino in Kenya:_ they are doomed to extinction with only two females (and no males) left in their entire species. _The Orangutans in Borneo and Sumatra:_ displaced and threatened due to massive expansions of palm oil plantations. There are so many more stories like these.**If we don't do something, things are only going to get worse.** That's why I've led the battles to take on trafficking and save some of the world's most iconic and endangered animals. [**Stand with me as we take on these overlapping crises. Donate today.**](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2f155/6cd27893/53f83a84/1586851b/1654793680/VEsF/p/eyJlM3RoYzJ0OWZRPT0iOiIzIiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjl2Ym1WOWZRPT0iOiI1IiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjkwZDI5OWZRPT0iOiIxNSIsImUzdGhZbUZ0ZEY5MGFISmxaWDE5IjoiMjUiLCJlM3RoWW1GdGRGOW1iM1Z5ZlgwPSI6IjUwIiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjltYVhabGZYMD0iOiIxMDAifQ%3D%3D/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/stand-with-jeff-merkley?refcode=2020-02-19-wildlife-e&recurring=1&amount=3&amounts=5,15,25,50,100") Donald Trump denies climate chaos and his sons have gleefully posed for pictures with the defenseless animals they've slaughtered. Those are _not_ the examples we need right now. **That's why I've introduced and passed legislation to fight wildlife trafficking** , and why I'm _always_ fighting to address the looming challenges of climate chaos. [**Help me keep up the fight. Donate today.**](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2f155/6cd27893/53f83a84/1586851b/1654793680/VEsC/p/eyJlM3RoYzJ0OWZRPT0iOiIzIiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjl2Ym1WOWZRPT0iOiI1IiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjkwZDI5OWZRPT0iOiIxNSIsImUzdGhZbUZ0ZEY5MGFISmxaWDE5IjoiMjUiLCJlM3RoWW1GdGRGOW1iM1Z5ZlgwPSI6IjUwIiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjltYVhabGZYMD0iOiIxMDAifQ%3D%3D/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/stand-with-jeff-merkley?refcode=2020-02-19-wildlife-e&recurring=1&amount=3&amounts=5,15,25,50,100") It's up to us. Let's _be_ the change on this critical issue. **Let's look out for**** _every_****creature that calls our planet home, and give our children and grandchildren a better tomorrow.** Onward. **_Jeff_** Express Donate: If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly: | [$5](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2f155/6cd27893/53f83a84/15868518/1654793680/VEsD/p/eyJlM3RpZFhSMGIyNWZiMjVsZlgwPSI6IjUifQ%3D%3D/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/stand-with-jeff-merkley?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=2020-02-19-wildlife-e&refcode2=express-buttons") | [$15](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2f155/6cd27893/53f83a84/15868519/1654793680/VEsA/p/eyJlM3RpZFhSMGIyNWZkSGR2ZlgwPSI6IjE1In0%3D/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/stand-with-jeff-merkley?express_lane=true&amount=15&refcode=2020-02-19-wildlife-e&refcode2=express-buttons") | [$25](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2f155/6cd27893/53f83a84/15868516/1654793680/VEsB/p/eyJlM3RpZFhSMGIyNWZkR2h5WldWOWZRPT0iOiIyNSJ9/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/stand-with-jeff-merkley?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=2020-02-19-wildlife-e&refcode2=express-buttons") | [$50](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2f155/6cd27893/53f83a84/15868517/1654793680/VEsO/p/eyJlM3RpZFhSMGIyNWZabTkxY24xOSI6IjUwIn0%3D/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/stand-with-jeff-merkley?express_lane=true&amount=50&refcode=2020-02-19-wildlife-e&refcode2=express-buttons")

subject: Did you see that?
body:
Last night’s Democratic debate showed exactly how much is at stake this election. | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/966676686457/exclusive-new-ad-alert-6358811?e=5ac6ff05c3)
---
| |
---
| | Last night’s Democratic debate showed exactly how much is at stake this election. In a nutshell, we heard candidate after candidate promise to roll back President Trump’s agenda, which would produce devastating results for the American people. **President Trump’s agenda has put America on a winning path.** Unfortunately, the president has had to advance his agenda _without the support of our congressman_ , who worked to block Trump’s attempt to repeal Obamacare, block Trump’s border wall proposal, and block Trump’s attempt to defund Planned Parenthood. **We need a representative who will vote YES on President Trump’s agenda.** That’s why I’m reaching out to ask for your support. [Your generous contribution of $100, $50, or even $25 will help](https://triumphcampaigns.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4871957391bbb49d4a9c3033e&id=9eed25bdcf&e=5ac6ff05c3) make sure Kentucky’s 4th congressional district is represented by someone who will fight with Trump, not against him. Thanks for considering a donation! Sincerely,
_**Todd McMurtry**_
P.S. I hope you know how grateful I’d be for you to consider a contribution. Every bit helps! [CLICK HERE](https://triumphcampaigns.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4871957391bbb49d4a9c3033e&id=e9a09d0bd9&e=5ac6ff05c3) to help ensure Kentucky’s 4th congressional district delivers a YES vote on Trump’s agenda.

name: Holcomb Crew
subject: Hoosier small businesses have our back
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Eric Holcomb for Indiana
body:
Governor Holcomb has always fought for Hoosier small businesses. Now, they are returning the favor by fighting for him in 2020. | |
---
| [](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=3b0cff36e50b4845a618a2d5346fab83&_e=uj716ssm4nSKfrXZIqKvJXMcRcTLVuGgkE3WAqrVBWIqjYK8r4PW8dBQHRqUu4dVrgHfu1sqEOPfeZNJL3CP-DfIfBklaYr2QaZ_6yxRiel7Zyr_np9o0vQbRzAPs1iJner_yIXYmHxy9va-j2xN9ebdIjuoDD3B73UXnBIEWDyJfRWKzDc0pGlgCXO0YXD-ixtqq0Aj1Nu4sdZezlWLtgnFTz3zdXON9711VRjySbd_A81D9JdEJ044g3HkJkNeOblwfmiyB0OWRStBFvnOlQDzcuAZx2RcFtRBwXEz6OXmsgBKStj-misK3H11YrQA2JMnmwaiS1X2iJbY5bHff4U9CE_UgQERM8PXggoXgQAyq-XfqXcec12OnRAICrcHiykm4YOdSV-FWSskhsWxHNHT_OfI_ZM-8yTwjFiDKes%3D) |
---|---|---
Friend,

Governor Holcomb has always fought for Hoosier small businesses. Now, they are returning the favor by fighting for him in 2020.

The National Federation of Independent Business (NFIB) — a network of more than 12,000 small and independent businesses across Indiana — just endorsed Governor Holcomb for four more years of serving Hoosiers.
[ ](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=3b0cff36e50b4845a618a2d5346fab83&_e=uj716ssm4nSKfrXZIqKvJXMcRcTLVuGgkE3WAqrVBWIqjYK8r4PW8dBQHRqUu4dVrgHfu1sqEOPfeZNJL3CP-DfIfBklaYr2QaZ_6yxRiel7Zyr_np9o0vQbRzAPs1iJner_yIXYmHxy9va-j2xN9ebdIjuoDD3B73UXnBIEWDyJfRWKzDc0pGlgCXO0YXD-ixtqq0Aj1Nu4sdZezlWLtrkhfNwd-y_B7jgVXfLFyEAtGHOGK60a68keaaFEo32sAcjzLdFfryXXeVPvhaPqGxuU6J8Jkl8HWuM8w8xEEAPpg3QtQhoARD2K_A2mkeHuCkCKe9luh2QJPTkZSFjp31cfAHM8Xdp2SxbmX8kAXk3Wed2mHCB7weVpUNn6aYzv2Fmh9Dw-GTYUyBoJWP8fzBPsWyUXrrVKyAaDf9KX0o0%3D)
[ **Chip in $3 and join them in endorsing the Governor** ](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=3b0cff36e50b4845a618a2d5346fab83&_e=uj716ssm4nSKfrXZIqKvJXMcRcTLVuGgkE3WAqrVBWIqjYK8r4PW8dBQHRqUu4dVwOmdfhTCbCHZAMs3PayJ3PPKqdr9K-02NVVNyWG9Dgo2mNEheLWk30L68dYCcvFdRFu6hNIw498rGQHCPOBeyX8RwrvStL1Q31yOxUr4Ihhucd5k3GGP7P3WxxKmIUGq57CXn4WnycLVfSKTjVdeQNCmEl42pOrUpRSXki0pKTDdWYtGLz-IiHUIkGOh19gh3Kh2nhU0F_8LwGJpb5KSoXyJApHyju9ktfHAx_pJ6lpiTmpK8n_krc3IeRg7prLj6fSS5d-vwjKffxBLQ3jgpenFB4Tpr5Gw5PKZbKDmov_yG6mDz53R6BSszfMdYcFMytYyK8OV1-An8rvVYstj5b0SlzGbtHPS6XZ0KqiT81Q%3D)
**This is the second time the NFIB has endorsed Governor Holcomb. **

Their mission is to create jobs and help small businesses thrive in Indiana, and we’re grateful that Indiana’s small business owners and workers recognize the governor’s hard work to help them in their fight.
**_“Indiana’s economy is running on all cylinders, and that’s because of our small businesses. Entrepreneurs across the state have solidified Indiana’s reputation on the national stage as a top place to start a business, get a great job and support a family. I’m thankful for NFIB’s continued support, and I know together we’ll keep Putting People First and working to foster an environment that empowers our small businesses to do what they do best.”-_ Governor Eric Holcomb**
Over the last four years, Governor Holcomb has signed balanced budgets, helped more than 10,000 Hoosiers build skills and earn certificates for high-wage jobs, expanded apprenticeships across the state, invested $100 million in expanding broadband access to Indiana’s unserved areas and so much more.

_And he’s not done yet — he wants to continue to help Hoosier small businesses and workers by Putting People First for another four years. Will you help him?_
[ **Chip in $3 and Join them in endorsing the Governor** ](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=3b0cff36e50b4845a618a2d5346fab83&_e=uj716ssm4nSKfrXZIqKvJXMcRcTLVuGgkE3WAqrVBWIqjYK8r4PW8dBQHRqUu4dVwOmdfhTCbCHZAMs3PayJ3PPKqdr9K-02NVVNyWG9Dgo2mNEheLWk30L68dYCcvFdRFu6hNIw498rGQHCPOBeyX8RwrvStL1Q31yOxUr4Ihhucd5k3GGP7P3WxxKmIUGq57CXn4WnycLVfSKTjVdeQBCakOkyOk2sdmVlb3u9RI8XN_1eLYLvdX5GuGWDnwW6quYC209IJn11Car0FxACyPPVhCPEgmJ1wL8W4YzvPiZ0FhuU-jlID_95-r9h54xrqrWFw9MyOnQ2AUGaFPQs25c6XeKoNidWB9CAOEARCkVHroDDQtE3ms8Xq0aE4ieqJEeU98pAfxOD5XL9dRuXF_x1uSElcQd7z8iFEj5KuUI%3D)
Sincerely,

Holcomb Crew

name: Tom Steyer
subject: What happened last night
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TOM STEYER 2020]()
body:
| | | |
---
---
Friend, last night’s debate only helped one person — Donald Trump. Instead of focusing on how we can improve the lives of Americans and build a diverse coalition to beat Mr. Trump, there was a lot of bickering over policy discrepancies that won’t matter if we don’t win in November. We're not going to beat the criminal in the White House by running the same conventional playbook and ignoring the upcoming fight. **Through successfully building a billion-dollar business and then stepping away to focus on fighting corporate corruption in our communities, I know how to build the diverse coalition we need, and I know how to beat Mr. Trump on the economy. And that’s what we need to do to win.** Here’s the plan: You and I both know that this race is still wide open. We still have a lot of other voters and states to hear from, but we only have a short time left to show the rest of the country that our vision for America is the winning one — [_**this is our chance. This is the time to make sure you’re ready to vote, that your friends and family are ready, too.**_](http://clicks.tomsteyer.com/ls/click?upn=M5l2K6CF-2BTeSi2jZtEX6WDq0JgL4SZ9fws6CW-2B-2BU1P27IVU80cPGCTCtX6J8AKTsViuPYvw6olQPbozfSUHBgzBnIgvUuhaFq-2BKEnSg6rfY-2F135WZq32UzwsyJSxsEcRGPy2TGTnd05NK7LhYcW3iisEPnySNLH8T4tPaaxQt7mES9eyKmFSM9UJSykrxZCNQwcRgld804Osk39Rk70uftxCQl-2FMC7Se01RFPUcn4NDVtRKJGakUSH07c3o2u7oOV19c_XwU9lPq5-2Bze3gI64ohlV0cQwWAuBAU7ADIvj2CS7ymjI06VISPfJIPkjxFld-2BrrlsLfWBgSznyC9eEQeuE67ByW7-2F4rW5t238CYEo3QD0YcmOVfQHjuZWRaSfg2NrPTXYrbO5YdSOOzN8F1O-2FzHPrF2DsLDQtyWliG-2BWZ41PUYHAEfgBV30BUz-2BUezJG08M8ulv-2BOOzOD-2BQrtYFDYX3d-2BXZQZPxoz4oLOA9f5X7K0WqxjD2pXC3ecXnCYRQ4mAYmrTMqjqDsnRD2kidstgNk007nieu42DOUstNDdKVl-2F-2BcWGY9xtWNP5OLVedAskqYbM1n6WFb8AGOQ54JWSLRMqvYfBhv-2FaR1Q99RmFqG9J3y7foZYydrBxNWJuW91fgTyfWA-2BYZpv88f1y9iSGjan-2FQxBtWFfIBK3gLr2vrNMvhE0QGnqdQ75qizeWqCmrsDGvnZcGUxBys40yvdGaJ-2FrOw-3D-3D) — Tom

clean_body: | | | | Friend, last night’s debate only helped one person — Donald Trump. Instead of focusing on how we can improve the lives of Americans and build a diverse coalition to beat Mr. Trump, there was a lot of bickering over policy discrepancies that won’t matter if we don’t win in November. We're not going to beat the criminal in the White House by running the same conventional playbook and ignoring the upcoming fight. Through successfully building a billion-dollar business and then stepping away to focus on fighting corporate corruption in our communities, I know how to build the diverse coalition we need, and I know how to beat Mr. Trump on the economy. And that’s what we need to do to win. Here’s the plan: You and I both know that this race is still wide open. We still have a lot of other voters and states to hear from, but we only have a short time left to show the rest of the country that our vision for America is the winning one — — Tom | | | "Facebook " | "Twitter " | "Instagram " ---|---|--- | Thank you for being a part of this campaign! subject: Did you see this?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by MJ for Texas
body:
The deadline is just hours away and we need your help.

|

[](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/6489056a/7f51f7bc/4ddd9b4e/4ef1bee1/3880118386/VEsH/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/logo?refcode=emaillogo&amount=5")

| | | Deadline: MIDNIGHT
Goal: $22,000
Still needed: $11,437

We still have $11,437 left to raise with just hours left before the deadline -- [can you read this message and chip in so we have the resources to fund this critical ad buy?](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/6489056a/7f51f7bc/4ddd9b4e/4ef1bee0/3880118386/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6Ijk0NCJ9/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-feb-wiring-deadline?refcode=email_20200220.2nd__944__&amount=10&recurring=true")
---
\---------- Forwarded Message ----------
From: **MJ Hegar Campaign HQ**
Subject: our new TV ad!
> [](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/6489056a/7f51f7bc/4ddd9b4e/4ef1bee0/3880118386/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6Ijk0NCJ9/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-feb-wiring-deadline?refcode=email_20200220.2nd__944__&amount=10&recurring=true")
> ---
> This is URGENT, Peter, so we'll get straight to the point: We just launched our new TV ad to reach undecided voters before the Texas primary, but we still need to raise $22,000 before tonight's wiring deadline!
>
> **[Will you rush a donation right now to make sure we have the resources to fund this critical ad buy?](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/6489056a/7f51f7bc/4ddd9b4e/4ef1bee0/3880118386/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6Ijk0NCJ9/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-feb-wiring-deadline?refcode=email_20200220.2nd__944__&amount=10&recurring=true")**
>
> Sen. John Cornyn aired the first TV ads of the race _months_ ago, and the Koch network ran ads supporting his reelection campaign too. **But unlike Sen. Cornyn, we don 't have an army of corporate PACs ready to write checks at a moment's notice. [MJ is counting on your grassroots support to win this race!](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/6489056a/7f51f7bc/4ddd9b4e/4ef1bee0/3880118386/VEsD/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6Ijk0NCJ9/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-feb-wiring-deadline?refcode=email_20200220.2nd__944__&amount=10&recurring=true")**
>
> Thanks to you, we've come a long way -- but now **the Texas primary is just around the corner** , and we urgently need your help to make sure MJ is the candidate who faces off against and BEATS Sen. Cornyn.
>
> **So Peter, can we count on you to pitch in before the wiring deadline and help give us the resources to win the nation 's LARGEST battleground state? **
> _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_
> ---
> **[Express donate $5>>](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/6489056a/7f51f7bc/4ddd9b4e/4ef1bee7/3880118386/VEsA/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6Ijk0NCJ9/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-feb-wiring-deadline?express_lane=true&refcode=email_20200220.2nd__944__&amount=5")**

_“She will fight for the residents of South Florida all day, every day.”_

**Ileana’s kind words are deeply special to me.** Her relentless pursuit of freedom, her unshakeable will in the face of communist dictators, and her dedicated years of service are all part of a legacy that I hope to continue for the people of South Florida.

name: Cynthia Cano, Powered By People
subject: We operate a little differently
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BETO FOR AMERICA
body:
[1]Powered By People

Super Tuesday is just 12
days away.

For a lot of organizations — especially in Texas — that’s their cue to
start reaching out to corporate PACs, lobbyists, and special interests to
build up a war chest and help buy the election for their preferred
candidates.

We operate a little differently at Powered By People. This is our cue to
ramp up our state and nationwide organizing — to get back out there and do
the hard work of talking with voters and turning Texas blue — one doorstep
at a time.

We can out-organize TV and radio ads, but something like that requires A
LOT of resources. [ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/42333?refcode=ema20200220-79929&t=1019&akid=79929%2E444211%2E0uryiV ]Which is why I’m asking you to make a $12 donation —
12 days before Super Tuesday. We need to start preparing immediately in
order to compete with the GOP’s deep pockets.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/42333?refcode=ema20200220-79929&t=1020&akid=79929%2E444211%2E0uryiV ]Donate $12

We know our candidates will almost certainly be outspent. That’s fine.
Because I’ve seen what this team and this state are capable of when we
band together:

* In 2018, young voter turnout in early voting was up by 500%.
* We won more votes than any Democrat in state history.
* And nearly 1.9 million Latinos cast a ballot — that’s more than twice
as many as the previous midterms.

Now, two years later, we’re staring down one of the most important
election years in our lifetimes. And in order to win, we’re going to need
a lot more of that energy.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/42333?refcode=ema20200220-79929&t=1021&akid=79929%2E444211%2E0uryiV ]Can you make a $12 donation to Powered By People today? Your support —
just a few dollars at a time — is what fuels our work.

I’m so excited to be back out there with all of you, and so grateful for
all that you do to support this cause.

Thank you,

Cynthia Cano
Powered By People

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/42333?refcode=ema20200220-79929&t=1022&akid=79929%2E444211%2E0uryiV ]Donate

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to act. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://act.betoorourke.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1023&akid=79929%2E444211%2E0uryiV

PAID FOR BY BETO FOR AMERICA

body:

| | Hi Peter \-- The day after Trump's acquittal, he took to national television to defend his "perfect call" with Ukraine and attacked Adam Schiff, calling him a "vicious, horrible person." He declared the impeachment "evil," calling it "corrupt" and full of "dirty cops," "leakers and liars." Now he has retaliated against national security official Lt. Col. Vindman for his testimony, granted pardons to a slew of corrupt politicians and Republican donors, and is turning the DOJ into an arm of his re-election campaign. But according to a new Emerson poll, 8% of Democrats say they won't vote against Trump if their top Democratic candidate doesn't win the primary. And another 13% say it depends on who becomes the nominee. If more than 20% of Democrats stay HOME in November, Trump will be re-elected without a check on his presidency. We need to know where you stand tonight: | | **Will you vote in November to defeat Donald Trump?**

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: The ‘Trump Bump’ is real
body:
America’s slow recovery from the Great Recession gave left-wing “experts” a fresh talking point: “secular stagnation.” They excused low growth... | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=36fc3e2895&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • February 20, 2020
---
| | |
# **The ‘Trump Bump’ is real**

**America’s slow recovery** from the Great Recession gave left-wing “experts” a fresh talking point: “secular stagnation.” They excused low growth under former President Obama as a new normal. Lost factory jobs and low wages were merely a sign of the times.

**They were wrong**. The first 3 years of the Trump Economy prove that low growth is far from inevitable. Common-sense policies such as tax cuts, regulatory reform, and energy independence have coincided with a remarkable turnaround: As of the end of 2019, real GDP is 1.4 percent—or $260 billion—higher than economists projected.

**President Trump’s results** not only beat expert predictions—they also beat the Obama “expansion” by the metrics. There have been turnarounds or improvements in homeownership, prime-age labor force participation, manufacturing employment, labor productivity, and, crucially, net wealth for the bottom half of American households.

[READ: The Trump Boom reversed Obama’s “new normal”](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=545d539caf&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## **The American Dream is for everyone**

**A few hours ago,** President Trump gave a commencement address to graduates of the “Hope for Prisoners” program in Las Vegas, Nevada. Hope for Prisoners helps former inmates get back on their feet and reenter society after they’ve served their time.

**🎬**** ****[President Trump: “Your best days are just beginning!”](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=98f3be8424&e=3ee4985eea)**

**“We are here** to reaffirm that America is a nation that believes in redemption,” the President said. “We believe in second chances.”

**There’s** **no better****champion** for criminal justice reform than President Trump. At the end of 2018, he signed the historic First Step Act, which reformed outdated sentencing guidelines, expanded judges’ discretion in punishing non-violent offenders, and invested over $400 million in programs to rehabilitate prisoners before they reenter society.

**Now, “we began** a nationwide campaign to encourage businesses to expand Second Chance Hiring,” he said. “When we say Hire American, we mean ALL Americans.”

[MORE: Trump Economy benefits historically disadvantaged Americans](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=708164e2e0&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## **Video of the day: THANK YOU, farmers!**
---
| [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ea9fe60032&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| **Major trade deals** such as the NAFTA-replacing USMCA and the Phase One agreement with China are huge promises kept to America’s farmers. Yesterday, President Trump delivered on another: While visiting Bakersfield, California, he signed a memorandum to clear regulatory hurdles that stifle water infrastructure projects.

**“I just want to thank** our farmers,” the President said before heading out west this week. “You’re brilliant in every way. Few people could do what you do.”

**🎬**** President Trump:** [“America is a nation built by farmers”](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d19856ac89&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## Photo of the Day
---
|
---
| **President Trump disembarks Air Force One in Arizona** | February 19, 2020
---

Lost factory jobs and low wages were merely a sign of the times. They were wrong. The first 3 years of the Trump Economy prove that low growth is far from inevitable. Common-sense policies such as tax cuts, regulatory reform, and energy independence have coincided with a remarkable turnaround: As of the end of 2019, real GDP is 1.4 percent—or $260 billion—higher than economists projected. President Trump’s results not only beat expert predictions—they also beat the Obama “expansion” by the metrics. There have been turnarounds or improvements in homeownership, prime-age labor force participation, manufacturing employment, labor productivity, and, crucially, net wealth for the bottom half of American households. | | The American Dream is for everyone A few hours ago, President Trump gave a commencement address to graduates of the “Hope for Prisoners” program in Las Vegas, Nevada. Hope for Prisoners helps former inmates get back on their feet and reenter society after they’ve served their time. 🎬 “We are here to reaffirm that America is a nation that believes in redemption,” the President said. “We believe in second chances.” There’s no betterchampion for criminal justice reform than President Trump. At the end of 2018, he signed the historic First Step Act, which reformed outdated sentencing guidelines, expanded judges’ discretion in punishing non-violent offenders, and invested over $400 million in programs to rehabilitate prisoners before they reenter society. Now, “we began a nationwide campaign to encourage businesses to expand Second Chance Hiring,” he said. “When we say Hire American, we mean ALL Americans.” | | Video of the day: THANK YOU, farmers! | | Major trade deals such as the NAFTA-replacing USMCA and the Phase One agreement with China are huge promises kept to America’s farmers. Yesterday, President Trump delivered on another: While visiting Bakersfield, California, he signed a memorandum to clear regulatory hurdles that stifle water infrastructure projects. “I just want to thank our farmers,” the President said before heading out west this week. “You’re brilliant in every way. Few people could do what you do.” 🎬 President Trump: | | Photo of the Day | | President Trump disembarks Air Force One in Arizona | February 19, 2020 | | | | | | The White House 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW Washington, DC 20500 * USA · 202-456-1111

name: Mark Warner
subject: Our national security
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Mark Warner
body:
---
| | Derek --

When the people in our intelligence community go to work each day, they check their Republican and Democrat hats at the door. They work hard, often risking their lives, to keep our country safe. Their work has nothing to do with politics -- it's about protecting the country we all love.

That's why it's so alarming to me that the President just named a partisan operative and Trump loyalist with zero intelligence experience to lead the entire intelligence community. While I am committed to working with Republicans and Democrats alike to keep Americans safe from the myriad of global threats we face, frankly I find it disturbing that this President is prioritizing personal loyalty above our national security.

Even more alarming, this latest appointment appears to be part of a pattern of the President undermining the independence and integrity of our federal law enforcement and national security institutions. And by appointing an intelligence director on an "acting" basis, the President is intentionally trying to dodge the Senate's constitutional authority to approve or disapprove of critical national security positions.

I'm proud of the fact that, as Vice Chairman of the Senate Intelligence Committee, we've been able to work on a bipartisan basis to focus on keeping our country safe. Whether it's investigating Russia's attack on our democracy, protecting us from terrorism, or looking ahead to future challenges we'll face from adversaries like China, America's national security is far too important to pollute with partisan politics.

The brave public servants of the intelligence community, many of whom live or work in Virginia, must be able to speak truth to power. As a leader of the Intelligence Committee, and as your Senator, I want you to know that I will keep working to protect our intelligence community from political interference and to keep our country safe from those who would do us harm.

Sincerely,
Mark Warner | **[CONTRIBUTE](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d5e0/53d9897e/531e014e/1c89bdc1/1043620338/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjQ4MSJ9/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/warner-db?refcode=20200220_f_1_nd__481__&refcode2=cont&amount=5")**
---

|
---

Friends of Mark Warner
1490-5A Quarterpath Road #213
Williamsburg, VA 23185

name: Team Hick
subject: Some good news and bad news
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hickenlooper for Colorado
body:
|
#
Team -- we have some good news and bad news. Let us explain: **The good news:** Our race rating was just upgraded by one of the nation's leading political analysts -- putting us one step closer to victory! Another reminder that our campaign represents the Democratic Party's best chance to flip a Republican Senate seat this November. **The bad news:** Trump is in Colorado today raising outrageous sums of money for Cory Gardner. Tonight, they're hosting a rally together, and we fully expect the president to viciously attack John Hickenlooper. We have less than a few hours to reach our $250,000 emergency fundraising goal before Trump takes the stage tonight. We're counting on a whole lot of folks like you to step up right now to help us fight back. **Will you rush an urgent contribution of $10 or more to John Hickenlooper 's campaign before Trump takes the stage tonight? Any amount you can donate will go a long way. name: Tina Smith
subject: Well, here's one promise Donald Trump actually kept...
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota
body:
| [](https://action.tinaforminnesota.%3D/?g=YCjTcDpTVzRarKyUt8_RZpA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tsm-em200220-hick?refcode=em200220-hick&recurring=yes&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amount=10") Friend --

Both Donald Trump and Mike Pence are in Colorado to campaign for Republican Senator Cory Gardner and against my good friend John Hickenlooper, who is running to defeat him and flip this seat blue.

This is the beginning of a new effort to tear down John's growing campaign for U.S. Senate. There's no doubt in my mind that Trump will attack him personally and those attacks will be amplified by Mitch McConnell's dark money donor network.

The truth is, Trump and McConnell know Colorado is one of the top opportunities for Democrats to flip a Republican-held seat and key to taking back the majority. And political insiders like Nate Silver's _FiveThirtyEight_ and _POLITICO_ know it, too. That's why Trump promised to visit Colorado "a lot" -- and now, he's fulfilling that promise (hey, there's a first time for everything!).

**Listen, we have to help John fight back today.** Trump and McConnell's attack machine is breaking fundraising records left and right, and the only way John can counter them is by collecting as many grassroots donations as possible.

**Can I count on you to split an urgent donation of $10 or more to support John's campaign for U.S. Senate in Colorado and mine? # On the Campaign Trail with Troy and Heather
---
| | | It's been a busy week on the campaign trail for Troy and Heather. From the High Line to Kalispell, folks around the State are jumping onto Team Downing because they know that Troy is the only conservative who can beat the Democrat this November.

The Auditor's office needs a Businessman and Proven Job Creator like Troy Downing.
---
|
---
| Troy stopped in Cut Bank (the coldest spot in the nation)
---
| |
---
| Troy and Heather stopped in Missoula to talk with Peter Christian. Check out the Interview!
---
| [Listen Now](https://troydowning.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7f12ce154379a6c3af4424424&id=b7d65e63f8&e=a212bb87cc "Listen Now")

|

| In Dawson County

| _Troy's Quote of the Day_
"As a consumer protection agency, we will come down hard on bad actors while removing red tape and unnecessary regulation that make it harder for businesses to thrive and compete."

|

name: Democratic Primary Update
subject: Will you vote for the Democratic Nominee?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Democratic Governors Association
body:
| | |
---
The Democratic primary is heating up, Team. A lot of great candidates are running for president, but we're worried some people won't vote for the Democratic nominee if that person isn't their first-choice candidate.
We know the only way we'll defeat Trump is if Democrats show up to the polls in record numbers. **With so much at stake, we can 't afford to sit this election out. **
**If we 're going to take on Trump and the GOP, Democrats need to be united - so we need to hear from 2,000 Democratic voters before 11:59 p.m.: **
| | **Will you vote for the Democratic nominee?**
---
| | | [YES](http://links.dga.mkt6018.com/ctt?kn=1&ms=MTY5ODgyOTgS1&r=NDgzNzk3NjA4NzczS0&b=0&j=MTgyMTI5MjY4MgS2&mt=1&rt=0)

* * *
Mitch McConnell has been damaging our democracy for too long. The damage he’s done will last for long after he leaves the Senate, and that’s precisely why he needs to lose the position of Senate Majority Leader as soon as possible. **_Here are 3 Reasons Mitch McConnell needs to go:_**
1. He violated the U.S. Constitution when he refused to allow a vote for months on President Obama’s Supreme Court nominee!
2. He was the architect of Trump’s sham impeachment trial in the Senate!
3. And under his leadership, he’s personally killed hundreds of bills such as background checks, increasing the minimum wage, fighting climate change, and many more!!!

_Enough is enough, Mitch McConnell!_ **[Donate if you’re ready to flip the U.S. Senate and start undoing the damage Mitch McConnell has had on our nation. We can’t let this wait any longer.](http://links.wilmotcollins.com/u/click?_t=9d604abc9de24d9a90a97f1ff916f905&_m=c2c72a5a1df44a9b90945eae92e0c28e&_e=9nWUDKBq0JpD5EYFEXGw6DXKbOJjsebpF61nBz8eLhRDugW4WKhJ4urPA8yo6CExHDC9dObnrJteWMgcVUpqPx2sZdxZUx0982_jOd-Jk-i8R287CnF0oAcqr9EY9YBvNj4QYwRH3t2yxoaRps4RGkVhQMC0yn75recWo_65UkdiYphs6tz5fqEclEvE6iEHpVEKkREQiOHpIqoheo15nQOtag2kbmbSDY28qu2ecdoR_6KKRb5EEtawgQbVnt7PHTzi3MFJYWAwpp1G9yrvxmOJmHoWaAHkxiQjlAmJMVAE6QnRwAVbHc0g1AlHEFljcWfG0n7BgDykFptD7iiAh8OF_uRTiE1E6c24tdoOPYVxSj8YA_QUGvgaoMLruE2DHE5ZLtROya0YUuz8gnrdbzST8Qsfrkgg53wVF0heh8HBddHHHHwl3uhLmpfKyJda6h1ZTjvKtpJjiNBwc8hIuszUrm0tEDTRnrDIj6KuRkY%3D)** \-- Wilmot Collins for Senate

Wilmot Collins is Montana’s first black mayor, a 20+ year military Veteran, a refugee and now, a U.S. Senate candidate. Wilmot will prioritize fighting climate change, making healthcare a human right, economic opportunity, supporting our veterans, improving our public schools and keeping public lands in public hands. [CONTRIBUTE MONTHLY](http://links.wilmotcollins.com/u/click?_t=9d604abc9de24d9a90a97f1ff916f905&_m=c2c72a5a1df44a9b90945eae92e0c28e&_e=9nWUDKBq0JpD5EYFEXGw6DXKbOJjsebpF61nBz8eLhRDugW4WKhJ4urPA8yo6CExHDC9dObnrJteWMgcVUpqPx2sZdxZUx0982_jOd-Jk-i8R287CnF0oAcqr9EY9YBvNj4QYwRH3t2yxoaRps4RGkVhQMC0yn75recWo_65UkdiYphs6tz5fqEclEvE6iEHpVEKkREQiOHpIqoheo15nQOtag2kbmbSDY28qu2ecdoR_6KKRb5EEtawgQbVnt7Peg4O_dkvJajBiuw9fVfaZk4QIYln5BekeEzfz53fj1i8dMKvBqv85efVpFr1JJqPMbb1-w8BXS5LQaz-43zEbrTBY7jYf7Y5VTji6V_Ip88lqOHiy_Mg4JRn7UH_XK1GkcnxHEYX-kEjF-zUndpZQW3sYISAqC-0TYXWZM4OGX7H4bqIBSm4R5rgTIrit98faGiXaKRegP5yBBmR6xd2R4bevK_zZC_rS7gi6o9KW_oPAv1DsW1pVB0p-SPQ1ayD)
